topic: LLS Suppression — Bucket Resolution Strategy
summary:
## Purpose
Prevents `LiveLoggingSystem` (LLS) from dominating bucket-entity resolution in the unified viewer. The primary root cause — an inverted `capturedBy` edge direction in the BFS traversal code in `ancestry.ts` — is fixed, eliminating incorrect LLS fallback in the majority of non-empty buckets. Option B2 suppression (`noiseAncestors` set) provides defense for residual edge cases driven by the data-level defect (missing semantic-content edges on Observation nodes).

## Architecture
- **Root cause (BFS traversal bug — fixed)**: the BFS walk in `ancestry.ts` was traversing `capturedBy` edges in the inverted direction; correcting the walk direction resolves the primary misresolution (was causing ~49/75 non-empty buckets to fall back to LLS)
- **Residual data defect (open)**: all Observation nodes carry only `capturedBy → LiveLoggingSystem`; semantic-content edges (`discusses`, `mentions`, `references`) are absent; when the data defect affects a bucket, even the corrected BFS terminates at LLS and Option B2 suppression applies
- **Strategy — Option B2**: post-BFS filter removes entries matching `noiseAncestors` when at least one non-noise resolution also exists; does **not** suppress LLS when it is the sole result
- **`noiseAncestors` set**: code-defined set of entity names/IDs treated as noise; supports adding future noise-tier ancestors without changing suppression logic; defined in the ancestry resolution layer, not as runtime config
- **S1 behavior**: when the post-filter result set is empty, suppression is lifted and LLS is preserved as the focal entity — prevents blank UI state in all-Observation buckets
- **S2 behavior**: when suppression collapses a multi-Digest bucket to exactly one Insight, the viewer auto-drills directly to that Insight (bypasses `BucketCardList`)
- **Q3 (bidirectionality)**: suppression also applies during `useNodeToBucketsIndex` reverse index construction — clicking the LLS graph node does not illuminate all ticks when suppression is active

## Key Files
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/graph/ancestry.ts` — BFS traversal (corrected `capturedBy` edge direction); Option B2 post-BFS filter; `noiseAncestors` set definition
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/store/viewer-store.ts` — `useNodeToBucketsIndex` reverse index; bidirectional suppression applied during index construction
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/panels/coding/LslTimelineStrip.tsx` — `onTickClick` invokes resolution with suppression active; S2 auto-drill dispatch
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/panels/SidePanel.tsx` — S1 fallback display path; `isSingleFocalMode` interacts with suppressed result sets
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/graph/ancestry.test.ts` — coverage for BFS direction, suppression logic, and `useNodeToBucketsIndex` mapping

## Usage
Suppression activates automatically during bucket-entity resolution on every tick click in `onTickClick`. No user-facing toggle. The corrected BFS walk direction handles the primary resolution case; Option B2 suppression handles residual fallback-to-LLS cases caused by the data-level defect. To add entities to the suppression set, update `noiseAncestors` in `ancestry.ts`. The ingest-side fix (adding semantic-content edges) remains open — see Knowledge Graph Semantic Edges insight.

## Troubleshooting
- **LLS still dominates most buckets despite fix being merged**: verify `ancestry.ts` contains the corrected `capturedBy` walk direction — if the direction was re-inverted, 49/75+ non-empty buckets will fall back to LLS
- **LLS appears when other ancestors are present (BFS direction is correct)**: data-level defect case (missing semantic-content edges); verify `noiseAncestors` filter executes after full BFS traversal with at least one non-noise result present
- **LLS absent when it is the sole ancestor**: S1 fallback guard missing — suppression must be lifted when the filtered result set is empty
- **Clicking LLS graph node illuminates all ticks**: Q3 bidirectionality not applied to `useNodeToBucketsIndex` construction; verify index build applies the same noise filter as forward resolution
- **Auto-drill fires on buckets with multiple Insights**: S2 single-Insight check too broad — exactly one resolved Insight required before drilling
- **Blank/empty panel on all-Observation buckets**: S1 zero-result restoration path absent

metadata.scope: Bucket-entity resolution layer in the unified viewer: BFS traversal direction fix in `ancestry.ts` and Option B2 post-BFS noise filtering

The corrected BFS walk direction handles the primary resolution case; Option B2 suppression handles residual fallback-to-LLS cases caused by the data-level defect. To add entities to the suppression set, update `noiseAncestors` in `ancestry.ts`. The ingest-side fix (adding semantic-content edges) remains open — see Knowledge Graph Semantic Edges insight.

## Troubleshooting
- **LLS still dominates most buckets despite fix being merged**: verify `ancestry.ts` contains the corrected `capturedBy` walk direction — if the direction was re-inverted, 49/75+ non-empty buckets will fall back to LLS
- **LLS appears when other ancestors are present (BFS direction is correct)**: data-level defect case (missing semantic-content edges); verify `noiseAncestors` filter executes after full BFS traversal with at least one non-noise result present
- **LLS absent when it is the sole ancestor**: S1 fallback guard missing — suppression must be lifted when the filtered result set is empty
- **Clicking LLS graph node illuminates all ticks**: Q3 bidirectionality not applied to `useNodeToBucketsIndex` construction; verify index build applies the same noise filter as forward resolution
- **Auto-drill fires on buckets with multiple Insights**: S2 single-Insight check too broad — exactly one resolved Insight required before drilling
- **Blank/empty panel on all-Observation buckets**: S1 zero-result restoration path absent

topic: System Health Dashboard — Frontend Pages and Service Architecture
summary:
## Purpose
Browser-based dashboard providing live views of raw observations, daily digests, weekly insights, token usage, and system health cards — all sourced from host-side services through the port-3033 API backend.

## Architecture
Four primary page tiers:
- **Observations page** — raw per-exchange observations; `observation-card.tsx` and `observation-filters.tsx` are the key rendering components; `observation-card.tsx` renders an amber `↳ sub-agent` badge for rows sourced from nested agents; detection covers Claude sub-agents (path-based pattern `/.claude/projects/` in `source` field) and OpenCode sub-agents (sqlite prefix in `source`); ~109 of 200 recent rows carry this badge
- **Digests page** (`src/pages/digests.tsx`) — daily thematic consolidation view; sticky consolidation error banner auto-dismisses whenever a successful `/api/consolidation/status` poll response is received
- **Insights page** (`src/pages/insights.tsx`) — weekly persistent knowledge synthesis; uses `MarkdownText` component at line 72 to render `insight.summary`; **open issue**: `##` headers not rendering correctly; **confirmed bug**: the "Update" (resynthesize) button and its associated Retry action send `POST /api/insights/<id>/resynthesize` to port 3033 (health API) instead of port 12436 (obs-api) — a server-side fix has been deployed to `observations-api-server.mjs`, but the 404 persists because the root cause is client-side routing in `insights.tsx`; fix requires redirecting that call to the obs-api base URL on port 12436
- **Token Usage page** (`src/pages/token-usage.tsx`) — Recharts visualisation including treemap; a **module-level 0.5% threshold constant** gates model visibility — models with fewer than 0.5% of total window tokens are hidden from **both** the Evolution chart legend and the "By Model" table (single source of truth for filtering); **open issue**: treemap has no `Tooltip` (smaller boxes non-interactive); **open issue**: 8 model name spelling variants from 3 uncanonicalized callsites in `server.mjs`
- **LLM Routing Settings dialog** (`src/pages/token-usage-settings-dialog.tsx`) — flex-column layout with pinned header/footer and overflow-scrollable middle

**Status mapping**: `mapCheckStatus` maps sub-check status strings to display states; `'unknown'` must map to neutral "Unknown", not "Offline"; `toUiStatus` in `server.js` must recognise `'passed'` as valid.

**Redux state**: `network` field must be declared in Redux state shape and populated from API response.

**Network/proxy badges**: text-only (`[N:HOME]`/`[N:CN]`, `[P:ON]`/`[P:OFF]`) — emoji avoided due to width rendering bugs.

Port assignments (inside `coding-services` Docker container):
- **3030** — constraint-dashboard Next.js frontend (host-only in dev mode)
- **3031** — dashboard API / `dashboard-server.js` (constraint dashboard backend)
- **3032** — system health dashboard UI (frontend)
- **3033** — system health dashboard API backend
- **3848** — retrieval / embedding service endpoint
- **8080** — container health check endpoint
- **12436** — obs-api; owns `POST /api/insights/<id>/resynthesize` and related observation/insight mutation endpoints

**Redaction token rendering**: `renderWithRedactionStyling` helper in `markdown-text.tsx` wraps `<USER_ID_REDACTED>` tokens in styled `<span>` elements.

**Visual confirmation workflow**: use `gsd-browser` for screenshot-based rendering confirmation; preference recorded in `memory/feedback_dashboard_screenshots_gsd_browser.md`.

## Key Files
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/observations.tsx` — observation list page
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/components/observation-card.tsx` — per-observation rendering; amber `↳ sub-agent` badge
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/components/observation-filters.tsx` — filter controls
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/digests.tsx` — Digests tier; error-state reset on successful consolidation status poll
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/insights.tsx` — Insights tier; `MarkdownText` at line 72; resynthesize/Retry button currently routes to wrong port (see Troubleshooting)
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/token-usage.tsx` — Token Usage; **module-level 0.5% threshold constant** shared by Evolution chart and By Model table; treemap (no `Tooltip`)
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/token-usage-settings-dialog.tsx` — LLM routing settings; flex-column pinned layout
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/components/system-health-dashboard.tsx` — main dashboard; `mapCheckStatus`
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/components/markdown-text.tsx` — `MarkdownText`; `renderWithRedactionStyling`
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/store/` — Redux state; `network` field must be declared
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/server.js` — API backend (port 3033)
- `memory/feedback_dashboard_screenshots_gsd_browser.md` — workflow preference: use `gsd-browser` for visual confirmation

## Usage
Dashboard frontend served on port 3032; API backend on port 3033. Constraint dashboard API on port 3031; constraint frontend on port 3030 (host only). Obs-api on port 12436 owns insight/observation mutation endpoints including resynthesize. Health data proxied from host coordinator at `HEALTH_COORDINATOR_URL`. Rebuild the Next.js frontend after schema or component changes. For visual confirmation of rendering changes, use `gsd-browser` rather than ad-hoc Playwright scripts. The 0.5% model visibility threshold in `token-usage.tsx` is a module-level constant controlling both Evolution chart and By Model table — adjust it in one place to affect both views. When adding observation-rendering features, use `observation-card.tsx` as the component entry point and verify the `source` field patterns for correct sub-agent badge detection.

## Troubleshooting
- **"Update"/"Retry" button on Insights page returns 404 on `POST /api/insights/<id>/resynthesize`**: The request is routed to port 3033 (health API) — this endpoint lives on port 12436 (obs-api). A server-side fix has been deployed to `observations-api-server.mjs`, but the 404 persists because the root cause is client-side: the button/Retry action in `insights.tsx` is not properly routing to or re-executing against port 12436. Two independent investigations confirmed the server fix alone is insufficient; the fix must redirect the resynthesize call in `insights.tsx` to the obs-api base URL on port 12436
- **Sub-agent badges absent from visible viewport**: Verify which time window is displayed — badged rows may exist earlier in the session; scroll up before investigating pipeline or data issues
- **Sub-agent badge not appearing on known sub-agent rows**: Verify `source` field contains either a `/.claude/projects/` path segment (Claude) or a sqlite prefix (OpenCode); check `observation-card.tsx`
- **Observation filters not applying**: Verify filter values are serialized in the fetch call
- **Container running but services inaccessible**: Force-recreate via `docker compose up --force-recreate`
- **Port 3033 blocked by host dashboard process**: Kill the conflicting host-side process (`lsof -i :3033`) then force-recreate
- **Consolidation error banner remains after API recovers**: Verify `digests.tsx` clears error state on successful poll; hard-reload if running stale build
- **`##` headers not rendering in insights view**: `MarkdownText` component header parsing unresolved — investigate `markdown-text.tsx`
- **Minor models not visible in Token Usage views**: The 0.5% threshold applies to both Evolution chart and By Model table — this is intentional; adjust the module-level threshold constant in `token-usage.tsx` if needed
- **Model missing from both views despite significant usage**: Check model-name canonicalization at the 3 uncanonicalized callsites in `server.mjs` — variant spellings may cause models to appear under names excluded by the threshold filter
- **Treemap boxes not interactive / no tooltip**: Add `Tooltip` to `token-usage.tsx`
- **Settings dialog clips content**: Verify flex-column overflow-scrollable layout in `token-usage-settings-dialog.tsx`
- **Network/proxy cards show "No data"**: Trace coordinator → `server.js` → Redux reducer → component; verify `network` field in Redux shape
- **Services showing "Offline" when status is unknown**: `mapCheckStatus` mapping `'unknown'` to `'offline'` — fix to neutral "Unknown"
- **Digests page shows count but empty body**: `project` column missing — re-run `migrate-add-project-column.js`

topic: Knowledge Context Injection — Retrieval Relevance and Deduplication
summary:
## Purpose
Ensures that the knowledge injected into agent prompts is topically relevant, non-redundant, and favors recently-verified insights — addressing historically poor signal-to-noise ratios caused by compressed MiniLM embedding scores, broken dedup logic, and absence of freshness weighting.

## Architecture
- **Embedding similarity compression**: MiniLM cosine scores cluster in the 0.75–0.82 range, making topic discrimination unreliable by score alone; Qdrant topic pre-filtering is the primary relevance gate
- **Topic pre-filtering**: `knowledge-injection-hook.js` extracts topics from the current conversation transcript and passes them to the retrieval service to pre-filter Qdrant results before RRF fusion
- **Reranking pipeline** (in `src/retrieval/retrieval-service.js`):
  1. `_applyContextBoost` — existing context-relevance boost step
  2. `_applyFreshnessRerank` — **newly added**; applies freshness decay (recency of last insight verification) and topic relevance scoring; stale or off-topic insights rank lower regardless of raw similarity score
- **Known gap**: `verificationRatio` (per-insight correctness score) does not yet influence retrieval ranking or confidence decay; `_applyFreshnessRerank` addresses recency but not accuracy weighting
- **Near-duplicate dedup** (injection-time, via `token-budget.js`): pairwise cosine similarity at **≥ 0.93** threshold before injecting insights; MiniLM cosine floor is ~0.89–0.92, making 0.90 too aggressive
- **Digest dedup**: `scripts/dedup-digests-by-embedding.js` at **0.97** threshold (high threshold required due to shared project vocabulary causing false positives at lower values)
- **Insight dedup**: `scripts/dedup-insights-by-embedding.js` — greedy pairwise merging at **≥ 0.93** (not single-linkage chaining, which causes transitive merges of unrelated items)
- **One-time cleanup results**: insights reduced 19 → 16; digests reduced 298 → 251

## Key Files
- `src/retrieval/retrieval-service.js` — reranking pipeline; `_applyContextBoost` followed by `_applyFreshnessRerank` (freshness decay + topic relevance)
- `knowledge-injection-hook.js` — topic extraction and Qdrant pre-filter call
- `token-budget.js` — per-tier caps and near-duplicate dedup at injection time (0.93 threshold for insights)
- `scripts/dedup-insights-by-embedding.js` — on-demand insight dedup; greedy pairwise merging
- `scripts/dedup-digests-by-embedding.js` — on-demand digest dedup; 0.97 threshold

## Usage
Topic filtering and freshness reranking run automatically on every hook invocation. `token-budget.js` dedup fires at injection time on every session start. Dedup scripts are run on-demand against the live DB when duplicate accumulation is suspected (after bulk consolidation runs or large data imports).

## Troubleshooting
- **Same insight injected twice at different confidence scores**: `token-budget.js` near-duplicate dedup not active or threshold too low — verify 0.93 threshold is in effect
- **Stale insights ranking above recently-verified ones**: `_applyFreshnessRerank` not wired or verification timestamps not being written to insight records — confirm the method is called after `_applyContextBoost` in `retrieval-service.js`
- **Unrelated insights merged after running dedup script**: Script using single-linkage chaining — switch to greedy pairwise merging in `dedup-insights-by-embedding.js`
- **Threshold 0.90 merging non-duplicates**: MiniLM cosine floor is 0.89–0.92; use ≥ 0.93 for insights and 0.97 for digests
- **Noise still high after topic filtering**: Qdrant may be non-functional (falling back to keyword search) — verify Qdrant health and container status; check retrieval service logs on port 3033/3848
- **`verificationRatio` not influencing results**: Accuracy-weighted ranking is a known remaining gap; `_applyFreshnessRerank` covers recency only — `verificationRatio` weighting is a future improvement

metadata.topic: Knowledge Context Injection — Retrieval Relevance and Deduplication

topic: Knowledge Context Injection — Hook and Agent Adapters
summary:
## Purpose
Provides a lightweight, token-efficient codebase exploration capability by offloading file search and structural analysis to a dedicated sub-agent with an isolated context window, preserving the primary `copilot` agent's token budget for higher-value work.

## Architecture
- **Spawn mechanism**: The `copilot` agent spawns one or more `explore` sub-agent instances via the Claude Agent SDK; each runs in a fully independent context window
- **Toolset**: `grep`, `glob`, `view` (read-only file inspection); expanded from March 6 onward to also include `bash`/`powershell` for safe shell-based search
- **Response cap**: 300 words per query — enforces focused, concise output
- **Initialization time**: typically 9–30 seconds per instance
- **Parallelism**: sub-agents are frequently spawned in bursts (multiple near-simultaneous instances); overlapping lifecycles confirm concurrent parallel dispatch, likely fanning out independent codebase <USER_ID_REDACTED>
- **Output scope**: read-only — no files are created or modified in any invocation; all output is returned to the calling agent context

**Known anomalies:**
- Repeated rapid spawning (e.g., 9 instances within 3 minutes, or 30 spawns in a day) may indicate retry loops, parallel fan-out, or orchestration misconfiguration — examine whether a single underlying query is triggering retries
- One confirmed spawn failure (15 seconds after initialization, no result returned); all other observed instances succeeded
- Near-simultaneous double-spawns (~300 ms apart) are common and may be duplicate triggers or intentional parallel instances

## Key Files
No project source files are modified by sub-agent execution. Configuration and orchestration are internal to the Claude Agent SDK / copilot agent runtime. Sub-agent lifecycle events appear in agent logs with spawn/complete timestamps.

## Usage
Triggered on-demand by the `copilot` agent whenever structural codebase questions arise (file locations, code topology, import graphs, etc.). The calling agent receives the sub-agent's answer and proceeds — there is no persistent state between sub-agent invocations. A single exploration session may spawn multiple sub-agents concurrently to answer independent questions in parallel.

## Troubleshooting
- **High-frequency burst of spawns (>5 in a minute) with no substantive output recorded**: May indicate a retry/orchestration loop rather than genuine parallel <USER_ID_REDACTED> — inspect the calling agent's task context and check whether error handling is triggering repeated re-spawns
- **Sub-agent fails ~15 seconds after initialization**: Likely a quota, timeout, or transient connectivity issue; retry is the appropriate response; if failures cluster in time, check proxy/LLM backend health
- **Sub-agent initialized but answers appear truncated**: 300-word cap is enforced; rephrase <USER_ID_REDACTED> to be more specific, or accept that multiple sub-agent calls may be needed for complex structural questions
- **Spawn overhead exceeding expectation**: Initialization takes 9–30 seconds per instance; for latency-sensitive workflows, pre-warm or accept the startup cost as a fixed overhead

metadata.topic: Knowledge Context Injection — Hook and Agent Adapters

topic: MkDocs Documentation — Directory Layout and Screenshot Placement
summary:
## Purpose
Defines where documentation source files, images, and diagrams must live to build correctly under MkDocs with `--strict` mode, provides a canonical style reference for writing new content, and tracks the diagram inventory across health monitoring, proxy, and network configuration topics.

## Architecture
**Directory layout:**
- `mkdocs.yml` sets `docs_dir: docs-content` — **all** source files (Markdown, images, PlantUML PNGs) must live under `docs-content/`, not `docs/`
- `docs-content/` — active MkDocs documents including: `network-configuration`, `status-line`, `llm-cli-proxy`, `health-monitoring`, `token-usage`, and `guides/network-configuration.md`
- `docs/` — legacy counterparts; not served by MkDocs but preserved as reference
- `docs-content/images/` — correct location for screenshots and diagrams; placing images in `docs/images/` causes MkDocs to silently ignore them
- `--strict` mode is used during builds; broken internal links and broken image references are fatal errors

**PlantUML diagram inventory and authoring notes:**
- Source files live under `docs/puml/`; rendered PNGs must be in `docs-content/images/` to appear in MkDocs builds
- **Original 3 scenario diagrams** (`network-overview.puml`, `network-macos-scenarios.puml`, `network-windows-scenarios.puml`) and their PNGs: deleted from the public repo working tree; preserved in the `coding-history` private GHE repo
- **4 new English-language network scenario diagrams** committed to `coding-history` under `docs/puml/` and `docs/images/`:
  - `network-scenario-inside-cn.puml/png`
  - `network-scenario-vpn.puml/png`
  - `network-scenario-no-vpn.puml/png`
  - `network-scenario-hotspot.puml/png`
  - These cover all 4 OS environments, HTTPS/SSH protocol flows, Kerberos/NTLM/OAuth auth, PAC→local→central proxy chain, firewall blocking rules, env var configuration, and a purple health-monitoring layer; **not yet copied to `docs-content/images/`** — placing PNGs in `docs/images/` means they do not appear in MkDocs builds
- Sequence diagrams exist covering: health monitoring auto-heal flow (including LLM proxy kickstart step), proxy auto-heal
- `docs/puml/km-core-unified-architecture.puml` — cross-system km-core architecture diagram; actively edited
- **`map` syntax limitation**: PlantUML `map` only supports 2-column key-value pairs — use `card` with creole formatting or `salt` widgets for multi-column layouts
- **ASCII box-drawing characters in notes**: do not render correctly; use PlantUML-native constructs
- **Layout direction**: use hidden links between packages to force top-to-bottom vertical stacking of scenario groups

**Documentation audit status:**
- Overall verdict: **70% current / 20% stale / 10% missing**
- Critical gap: OKB system is fully self-documented internally but absent from main coding docs
- An 8-item prioritized punch list exists; highest-priority items target OKB integration visibility and stale path/port corrections

**Canonical screenshot filenames:**
- `log-insights.png`, `log-observations.png`, `log-digests.png`
- `observation-viewer.png`, `observation-viewer-item.png`
- `health-mon.png` — canonical health dashboard hero image; verify not degraded after prior rename from `heatlh-dashboard-hero.png`
- Obsolete names `digests-page.png`, `insights-page.png`, and `healtlh-dashboard-hero.png` (misspelled) — all references updated

**Style conventions (`documentation-style.md`, 226 lines):**
- PlantUML and Mermaid diagram authoring conventions
- Markdown formatting rules and common violations
- File placement rules (mirrors the `docs-content/` requirement)
- PNG generation commands for PlantUML diagrams

## Key Files
- `mkdocs.yml` — site configuration; sets `docs_dir: docs-content`
- `docs-content/` — all live documentation source files and assets
- `docs-content/guides/network-configuration.md` — guide covering proxy, health monitoring, and network configuration topics
- `docs-content/images/` — correct location for screenshots and diagrams
- `docs-content/images/health-mon.png` — health dashboard hero image; verify not in degraded state
- `docs/` — legacy documentation directory (not served by MkDocs)
- `docs/puml/` — PlantUML source files (original 3 network scenario PUMLs removed from public repo; health/proxy sequences remain)
- `docs/puml/_standard-style.puml` — shared style include for remaining diagrams
- `docs/puml/km-core-unified-architecture.puml` — cross-system km-core architecture diagram; actively edited
- `documentation-style.md` — 226-line style guide
- `coding-history` GHE repo `docs/` — contains both the original 3 and the 4 new English-language network scenario PUMLs and PNGs

## Usage
Build locally with `mkdocs serve` or `mkdocs build --strict`. New `.puml` diagrams must be accompanied by generated PNGs placed in `docs-content/images/`. Before writing new documentation, read `documentation-style.md`. To include the 4 new network scenario diagrams in MkDocs: copy PNGs from `coding-history/docs/images/` to `docs-content/images/` and add Markdown references.

## Troubleshooting
- **Build fails referencing original 3 network diagram PNGs**: Deleted from public repo — remove or update references; originals preserved in `coding-history`
- **New 4 network scenario PNGs not appearing in MkDocs**: PNGs are in `coding-history/docs/images/` — copy to `docs-content/images/` for MkDocs to serve them
- **`health-mon.png` appears degraded**: Rename from `heatlh-dashboard-hero.png` may have substituted a screenshot version — restore from git history or re-capture
- **Screenshots missing from deployed site**: Images placed in `docs/images/` instead of `docs-content/images/`
- **Build fails with broken link error**: Search `docs-content/` for the referenced filename; verify image names match the canonical list above
- **PlantUML `map` block not rendering multi-column table**: Use `card` with creole table syntax or `salt` widgets
- **Diagram scenarios render side-by-side instead of stacked**: Add hidden links between package blocks
- **OKB not visible in architecture/integrations docs**: Known critical gap — add to `core-systems`, `integrations`, and `data-flow` documents

topic: Health Coordinator — Phase 33 Consolidated Health Monitoring
summary:
## Purpose
Consolidates all service probing into a single coordinator process exposing health results and system-wide state over HTTP, eliminating split-brain between consumers and providing auto-healing for Proxydetox and the LLM proxy through a cascaded FSM.

## Architecture
- **Coordinator daemon**: `scripts/health-coordinator.js` — Express server on port **3034**; LaunchAgent plist; ~5 s tick for semantic probe scheduling; full network/proxy probe cycle at reduced interval
- **IMPORTANT — port 3033 ownership**: Port 3033 is owned by the `coding-services` Docker container as a port-forward. The coordinator listens on **3034** only.
- **Monitored endpoints**: `api.github.com`, `api.anthropic.com`, `localhost:12435` (LLM proxy), corporate DNS host
- **Network location classification**: `detect-network.sh` subprocess; 3-signal priority chain — VPN CLI (`stdin: 'ignore'`) → `dig`-based DNS probe → `open`; valid values: `corporate`, `vpn`, `open`
- **Proxydetox probe — three-state**: `P:ON` (port 3128 listening AND functional HTTP CONNECT test passes), `P:ERR` (zombie listener — port bound but CONNECT fails), `P:OFF` (port not listening)
- **Auto-heal FSM**: 2 consecutive failures trigger `launchctl kickstart`; max 3 restarts per 5 minutes; LLM proxy kickstart cascades on Proxydetox heal; `last_probe_end` reset to `null` forces immediate semantic re-probe (~10 s recovery window)
- **Semantic probes**: cheap `"say OK"` / `/ok/i` (~1 s); strong probe `pollProxySemanticStrong()` in parallel; result exposed as `semantic_strong_ok`; `setImmediate()` triggers strong probe when cheap flips `false → true`
- **LLM proxy routing**: coordinator must be routed to `copilot` provider in `processOverrides` — `claude-code` causes HTTP 500s

**Known data-mapping bugs (unresolved):**
- **LevelDB/Qdrant show "unknown" in dashboard**: `health-coordinator.js` (lines 1146–1201) stores PSM results under `levelDB`/`qdrant` object keys but the dashboard frontend reads rule-named string keys (`leveldb_lock_check`, `qdrant_availability`) — the mapping layer is entirely absent; default "unknown" is never overwritten
- **Stale PIDs check unimplemented**: `check_type: 'pid_alive'` is defined in `health-verification-rules.json` (lines 157–163) and wired in `health-remediation-actions.js` (line 135), but the coordinator has no dispatch logic for `pid_alive` — no liveness probing exists
- **`semantic_ok` vs dashboard mismatch**: `combined-status-line.js` (lines 1918–1961) derives `proxy.status` from `semantic_ok + auto_heal_status`; the dashboard frontend renders raw fields directly — divergent derivation causes yellow statusline indicator with no corresponding dashboard widget

## Key Files
- `scripts/health-coordinator.js` — coordinator daemon (port 3034); PSM result storage (lines 1146–1201, missing key mapping); `last_probe_end` reset; LLM proxy cascade; `dig`-based DNS; `POST /health/refresh`; `pollProxySemanticStrong()`
- `config/health-verification-rules.json` — rules; `pid_alive` defined but unimplemented; `bind_mount_freshness` deleted
- `scripts/detect-network.sh` — network location classifier
- `scripts/health-remediation-actions.js` — includes `restartObsApi`; `pid_alive` remediation wired but unreachable
- `rapid-llm-proxy/proxy-bridge/server.mjs` — `processOverrides` must route `health-coordinator` to `copilot`
- `scripts/combined-status-line.js` — derives proxy.status differently from dashboard (lines 1918–1961)
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/server.js` — reverse-proxies coordinator; `getProxyHealthItems` (lines 355–387)
- `scripts/proxy.sh` / `px` — post-hook calls `POST /health/refresh`

## Usage
Runs as a LaunchAgent on the host. All health consumers call port **3034**. Network/proxy state is authoritative. After any proxy restart, verify `processOverrides` routes `health-coordinator` to `copilot`. Restart coordinator after network changes to clear DNS staleness. CGR commits-behind (`git rev-list`) is computed in `server.js` (lines 562–627) but silently fails inside Docker where `.git` is not mounted — only the commit hash is visible in container environments.

## Troubleshooting
- **LevelDB/Qdrant sub-checks show "unknown"**: Key mapping layer missing in coordinator — PSM results stored under `levelDB`/`qdrant` keys but dashboard reads `leveldb_lock_check`/`qdrant_availability`; implement the mapping in `health-coordinator.js` lines 1146–1201
- **PID liveness check never fires**: `pid_alive` dispatch absent from coordinator tick logic — add a dispatch branch; config and remediation are already wired
- **CGR "commits behind" absent in dashboard (Docker)**: `.git` not mounted in container; `git rev-list` fails silently; mount `.git` read-only or accept commit-hash-only display
- **Yellow brain emoji in statusline but no matching dashboard widget**: `proxy.status` derivation differs between statusline and dashboard — align field logic in `combined-status-line.js` with dashboard's raw-field rendering
- **Proxydetox shows `P:ON` but traffic fails after VPN disconnect**: Zombie listener — functional HTTP CONNECT test should detect `P:ERR` and trigger auto-heal; verify CONNECT probe is deployed
- **Semantic probe HTTP 500 / 12 consecutive failures**: `processOverrides` routing `health-coordinator` to `claude-code` — switch to `copilot`
- **Sub-checks show "unknown"**: First probe cycle not complete — restart to force re-probe; or mapping bug (see above)
- **VPN CLI hangs coordinator**: `stdin` not set to `'ignore'` on `execFile` call
- **Launchd job exits immediately**: Use full absolute path to `node` in `ProgramArguments`

topic: Discuss-Phase Planning Workflow
summary:
## Purpose
The discuss-phase workflow is a structured pre-planning gate that resolves implementation decision points and scope boundaries via human input before any researcher or planner agent is dispatched. Its output artifact (`{phase}-CONTEXT.md`) is the authoritative handoff document that all downstream agents consume.

## Architecture
- **Pre-discussion audit (optional preparation step)**: a single-pass scan of planning artifacts surfaces all open deferred work and inter-task dependencies; documents scanned: `STATE.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, pending todos (`.planning/todos/pending/`), backlog phases, and DEFERRED sections across all planning documents; produces no artifact — findings feed directly into next-action prioritization and phase-scope selection
- **Mode routing**: the `discuss_mode` config field selects the workflow file:
  - **`discuss-phase-assumptions.md`** (assumptions-only): records stated assumptions and proceeds without interactive discussion
  - **`discuss-phase.md`** (full discussion): waits for user input to populate open decision points (gray areas)
- **Five-step execution sequence** (full discussion mode):
  1. Load prior context: `{n-1}-CONTEXT.md`, `STATE.md`, `PROJECT.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`
  2. Scout codebase for reusable assets from the preceding phase
  3. Identify unresolved gray areas only — decided questions are not re-litigated
  4. Facilitate targeted user discussion to resolve each gray area
  5. Write `{phase}-CONTEXT.md` capturing all locked decisions
- **Scope policy**: scope-creep items identified during discussion are explicitly deferred to later roadmap phases, not folded into current scope

## Key Files
- `~/.claude/get-shit-done/workflows/discuss-phase.md` — full-discussion mode workflow definition
- `~/.claude/get-shit-done/workflows/discuss-phase-assumptions.md` — assumptions-only mode workflow definition
- `PROJECT.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, `STATE.md` — loaded as input context at workflow start
- `ROADMAP.md` — scanned during pre-discussion audit for high-level roadmap gaps
- `.planning/todos/pending/` — scanned during pre-discussion audit for open task-level blockers
- `{n-1}-CONTEXT.md` — loaded at initialization for prior decision context only; not forwarded as the active handoff document
- `{phase}-CONTEXT.md` — output artifact; locked decisions consumed by all downstream researcher and planner agents

## Usage
Optionally preceded by a deferred-work audit. The discuss session is initiated at the start of planning for each new phase, before researcher or planner agents are dispatched. In full discussion mode the workflow waits for user input; in assumptions-only mode it proceeds with stated assumptions. Scope-creep is logged for later roadmap phases rather than absorbed into current scope.

## Troubleshooting
- **Competing deferred items without clear prioritization**: run the deferred-work audit before starting the discuss session to establish an inter-task dependency map
- **`CONTEXT.md` empty or missing after initialization**: workflow was initialized but user input was never provided — resume in full discussion mode using `discuss-phase.md`
- **Downstream agents on stale or wrong-phase context**: confirm the `CONTEXT.md` passed to agents is from the current phase; `{n-1}-CONTEXT.md` is for initialization only
- **Scope items appearing in implementation plans**: re-run or extend the discuss session with explicit scope boundaries before dispatching any planning agent
- **Wrong workflow file loaded**: check `discuss_mode` config; use `discuss-phase.md` when human input is required
- **Missing planning directories after any revert**: run a fresh `/gsd-discuss-phase` from scratch — never resume from a partially reverted state

topic: LSL Session Continuity Bootstrap
summary:
## Purpose
LSL files are the primary verbatim record of coding sessions. Loading recent LSL files at session start is the standard procedure to re-establish task state, in-progress work, and likely next steps without relying on memory or external summaries.

## Architecture
- **File storage**: LSL files are dated flat files in `.specstory/history/`. Sub-agent logs follow `YYYY-MM-DD-HHHH-HHHH_S1_<index>-<ID-HASH>`; main session LSLs use analogous date-stamped naming.
- **Two-tier content**: each LSL contains a verbatim transcript tier and may be accompanied by observation/summary records in the observations database.
- **Length-based detection threshold**: if the primary (most-recent) file is `< 50KB` or `< 500 lines`, additional historical files must be loaded. Note: the KB threshold may trigger even when line count exceeds 500 (e.g., a 707-line file can still be flagged as insufficient if it is small in bytes).
- **Aggregation minimum**: the four most recent files spanning the last 1–2 days is the recommended floor.
- **Load order**: oldest-to-newest — reversed order causes decisions to appear before their motivating context.
- **Cross-project resolution**: the protocol includes a cross-project context step, but this is skipped when the active project is already the target project (e.g., `coding`).

## Key Files
- `.specstory/history/` — active session log directory; all LSL markdown files for the current project
- `scripts/backfill-subagent-transcripts.mjs` — recovers sub-agent LSL content for gaps not captured live
- `.planning/phases/STATE.md` — complements LSL content with current phase implementation state
- `projects.json`, `defaultDir.ts` — used to resolve the exact `.specstory/history/` path per project

## Usage
Invoked at session start before any substantive work. Procedure: (1) query `.specstory/history/` for recent files; (2) apply length-based detection — if primary file is `< 50KB` or `< 500 lines`, pull additional historical files; (3) load files oldest-to-newest; (4) cross-reference `.planning/phases/STATE.md` for planning context. Read-only — no artifacts are modified during bootstrap.

## Troubleshooting
- **Insufficient context from a single file**: apply both thresholds (`< 50KB` AND `< 500 lines`); a file may pass the line count check but still be flagged by the size check — load additional files in either case
- **Bootstrap stalls mid-execution**: the directory-listing step may hang; re-invoke the query before proceeding
- **Narrative incoherence after loading**: verify oldest-to-newest load order; reversed order makes decisions appear before their antecedents
- **Task details not surfaced by LSL alone**: cross-reference `.planning/phases/STATE.md` and the relevant `*-CONTEXT.md` phase file
- **Sub-agent turns missing from LSLs**: run `backfill-subagent-transcripts.mjs` with a 1-day recency filter before treating the record as complete

- **Narrative incoherence after loading**: verify oldest-to-newest load order; reversed order makes decisions appear before their antecedents
- **Task details not surfaced by LSL alone**: cross-reference `.planning/phases/STATE.md` and the relevant `*-CONTEXT.md` phase file
- **Sub-agent turns missing from LSLs**: run `backfill-subagent-transcripts.mjs` with a 1-day recency filter before treating the record as complete

metadata.scope: Session startup protocol for loading prior LSL files to reconstruct task context before any substantive work begins.
metadata.topic: LSL Session Continuity Bootstrap
metadata.summary:
## Purpose
LSL files are the primary verbatim record of coding sessions. Loading recent LSL files at session start is the standard procedure to re-establish task state, in-progress work, and likely next steps without relying on memory or external summaries.

## Architecture
- **File storage**: LSL files are dated flat files in `.specstory/history/`. Sub-agent logs follow `YYYY-MM-DD-HHHH-HHHH_S1_<index>-<ID-HASH>`; main session LSLs use analogous date-stamped naming.
- **Two-tier content**: each LSL contains a verbatim transcript tier and may be accompanied by observation/summary records in the observations database.
- **Length-based detection threshold**: if the primary (most-recent) file is `< 50KB` or `< 500 lines`, additional historical files must be loaded. Note: the KB threshold may trigger even when line count exceeds 500 (e.g., a 707-line file can still be flagged as insufficient if it is small in bytes).
- **Aggregation minimum**: the four most recent files spanning the last 1–2 days is the recommended floor.
- **Load order**: oldest-to-newest — reversed order causes decisions to appear before their motivating context.
- **Cross-project resolution**: the protocol includes a cross-project context step, but this is skipped when the active project is already the target project (e.g., `coding`).

## Key Files
- `.specstory/history/` — active session log directory; all LSL markdown files for the current project
- `scripts/backfill-subagent-transcripts.mjs` — recovers sub-agent LSL content for gaps not captured live
- `.planning/phases/STATE.md` — complements LSL content with current phase implementation state
- `projects.json`, `defaultDir.ts` — used to resolve the exact `.specstory/history/` path per project

## Usage
Invoked at session start before any substantive work. Procedure: (1) query `.specstory/history/` for recent files; (2) apply length-based detection — if primary file is `< 50KB` or `< 500 lines`, pull additional historical files; (3) load files oldest-to-newest; (4) cross-reference `.planning/phases/STATE.md` for planning context. Read-only — no artifacts are modified during bootstrap.

## Troubleshooting
- **Insufficient context from a single file**: apply both thresholds (`< 50KB` AND `< 500 lines`); a file may pass the line count check but still be flagged by the size check — load additional files in either case
- **Bootstrap stalls mid-execution**: the directory-listing step may hang; re-invoke the query before proceeding
- **Narrative incoherence after loading**: verify oldest-to-newest load order; reversed order makes decisions appear before their antecedents
- **Task details not surfaced by LSL alone**: cross-reference `.planning/phases/STATE.md` and the relevant `*-CONTEXT.md` phase file
- **Sub-agent turns missing from LSLs**: run `backfill-subagent-transcripts.mjs` with a 1-day recency filter before treating the record as complete

topic: LSL Session Continuity Bootstrap
summary:
## Purpose
LSL files are the primary verbatim record of coding sessions in the km-core project. Loading recent LSL files at the start of a new session is the standard procedure to re-establish task state, in-progress work, and likely next steps without relying on memory or external summaries.

## Architecture
- **File storage**: LSL files are dated flat files in `.specstory/history/`. Sub-agent logs follow `YYYY-MM-DD-HHHH-HHHH_S1_<index>-<ID-HASH>`; main session LSLs use analogous date-stamped naming.
- **Two-tier content**: each LSL contains a verbatim transcript tier (raw turn content) and may be accompanied by observation/summary records in the observations database.
- **Length-based detection threshold**: if the primary (most-recent) file is `< 50KB` or `< 500 lines`, additional historical files must be loaded; a single short file is never sufficient alone.
- **Aggregation minimum**: the four most recent files spanning the last 1–2 days is the recommended floor; individual sessions are typically too short for sufficient context in isolation.
- **Timestamp-based filtering**: applied to identify files within the relevant recent time window when the directory contains many candidates.
- **Cross-project context resolution**: the protocol includes a resolution step for environments where multiple projects share history infrastructure.
- **Load order**: oldest-to-newest — reversed order causes decisions to appear before their motivating context.

## Key Files
- `.specstory/history/` — active session log directory; all LSL markdown files for the current project
- `scripts/backfill-subagent-transcripts.mjs` — recovers sub-agent LSL content for gaps not captured live
- `.planning/phases/STATE.md` — complements LSL content with current phase implementation state
- `projects.json`, `defaultDir.ts` — used to resolve the exact `.specstory/history/` path per project

## Usage
Invoked at session start before any substantive work. Procedure: (1) query `.specstory/history/` directory for recent files; (2) apply length-based detection — if primary file is `< 50KB` or `< 500 lines`, pull additional historical files; (3) load files oldest-to-newest; (4) cross-reference `.planning/phases/STATE.md` for planning context. Recovery goals: which tasks were active, what state each was left in, what next steps are implied. Read-only — no artifacts are modified during bootstrap.

## Troubleshooting
- **Insufficient context from a single file**: apply the length threshold — `< 50KB` or `< 500 lines` requires loading additional files before concluding context is established
- **Bootstrap stalls mid-execution**: the directory-listing bash step may return slowly or hang; re-invoke the query before proceeding to the load steps
- **Narrative incoherence after loading**: verify oldest-to-newest load order; reversed order makes decisions appear before their antecedents
- **Task details not surfaced by LSL alone**: cross-reference `.planning/phases/STATE.md` and the relevant `*-CONTEXT.md` phase file for structured task state
- **Sub-agent turns missing from LSLs**: run `backfill-subagent-transcripts.mjs` with a 1-day recency filter before treating the LSL record as complete

topic: obs-api Ontology Registry Sourcing Architecture
summary:
## Purpose
Documents the architectural split between obs-api's bundled ontology registry and the host-side ontology directory. L0 anchor classes (`System`, `Project`) are synthesized correctly by the km-core API handler at port 12436. L1/L2 class annotations added to `.data/ontologies/` remain invisible to `GraphKMStore`-backed queries and the unified viewer filter until a writer-validation-gated `ontologyDir` reroute is implemented (Phase 60.08/61, Deviation 3).

## Architecture
- **Default registry load path**: `GraphKMStore` reads ontology class definitions from the **bundled** `lib/km-core/ontology/` directory at startup
- **Host-side directory ignored**: `.data/ontologies/` is not consulted; L1/L2 classes annotated there are absent from all `GraphKMStore`-backed queries and viewer filter group headers
- **Bundled-only classes**: `LearningArtifact`, `Digest`, `Insight`, `Observation` exist in the bundled registry but are absent from the host `upper.json`; writer-side validation for these classes depends entirely on the bundled path remaining active
- **L0 synthesis unaffected**: the km-core API handler at port 12436 synthesizes `System` and `Project` L0 anchor classes correctly via `HIERARCHY_ROOTS` + `extends` fallback — this path bypasses the registry sourcing gap
- **L1/L2 blocked (expected)**: L1/L2 group headers absent from the viewer filter is the expected behavior under the bundled-registry constraint, not a handler defect
- **Fix location**: `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs` line 1336 — a single `ontologyDir` swap reroutes `GraphKMStore` to `.data/ontologies/`
- **Architectural gate**: the reroute requires a writer-validation audit confirming the four bundled-only classes are added to host `upper.json` before the swap; applying the one-liner without this audit orphans writer-side validation
- **Decision classification**: Rule-4 cross-cutting architectural decision; deferred to Phase 60.08 or 61
- **Current state**: Deviation 3 in `60-VERIFICATION.md`, status `PARTIAL (L0 lit; L1/L2 blocked)`; Phase 60-07 closed as partial-PASS

## Key Files
- `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs` — line 1336: `ontologyDir` configuration for `GraphKMStore`; the pending fix target
- `lib/km-core/ontology/` — bundled ontology registry; loaded by default; contains `LearningArtifact`, `Digest`, `Insight`, `Observation`
- `.data/ontologies/` — host-side ontology directory; contains newly annotated L1/L2 classes; **not** read by current obs-api
- `.data/ontologies/upper.json` — host-side L0/L1/L2 hierarchy; does **not** contain the four bundled-only classes
- `.planning/phases/60-unified-viewer-rendering-ux-integrity/60-VERIFICATION.md` — Deviation 3 record; current status `PARTIAL (L0 lit; L1/L2 blocked)`
- `.planning/todos/pending/2026-06-17-ontologyfilter-runtime-routing-gap.md` — follow-up phase draft for the reroute and writer-validation audit

## Usage
The bundled-registry behavior is intentional for writer-side class validation. L0 anchor classes are correctly synthesized by the km-core API handler. L1/L2 classes added to `.data/ontologies/` will not appear in viewer filters or `GraphKMStore` results until Phase 60.08/61 implements the reroute with an accompanying writer-validation audit. Do not apply the one-liner fix without first completing the audit and adding bundled-only classes to host `upper.json`.

## Troubleshooting
- **L1/L2 group headers absent from viewer filter**: expected; Deviation 3 is deferred to Phase 60.08/61; not a handler defect
- **L0 group headers (`System`/`Project`) absent from viewer filter**: unexpected; verify km-core API handler at port 12436 is running and `HIERARCHY_ROOTS` registry is compiled
- **`LearningArtifact`/`Digest`/`Insight`/`Observation` validation breaks after reroute**: absent from host `upper.json`; add them before or alongside the `ontologyDir` swap
- **Confirming which ontology path is active**: inspect line 1336 of `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs`; `lib/km-core/ontology/` = bundled (current default), `.data/ontologies/` = host (pending)
- **Deviation 3 re-evaluated as blocking**: operator options — (a) partial-PASS + Phase 60.08/61 follow-up [recommended], (b) inline fix with full writer-validation audit, (c) reject and triage

id: Knowledge Graph Viewers — VOKB and VKB
topic: Knowledge Graph Viewers — VOKB and VKB
summary:
## Purpose
Provides browser-based interactive visualizations of the operational knowledge graph, enabling developers to explore entities, relations, and graph topology. Three implementations: VOKB (D3/React), VKB (sigma.js), and the unified viewer. Phase 56 (bidirectional selection) and Phase 56.1 (many-to-many temporal-knowledge bridge) are complete; Phase 60 (`unified-viewer-rendering-ux-integrity`) is in progress with Wave 1 at 1/6 plans done; Phase 60.1 UX integrity issues are scoped and pending implementation.

## Architecture

**VOKB:** `rapid-automations/integrations/operational-knowledge-management/`; React 18 + D3.js + Vite + Tailwind CSS; port 3002.

**VKB:** sigma.js; port 8080 — verify no conflict with `coding-services` health check endpoint.

**Unified Viewer (`integrations/unified-viewer/src/`):**
- `D3GraphCanvas.tsx`: D3 force-simulation canvas; two-tier halo ring rendering (`focalNodeId` = primary ring, `selectedNodeIds` = co-selected ring); drill-collapse click flow; `nodeToBuckets` reverse index in `useRef`; no viewport centering (AC #3 retracted)
- `ancestry.ts`: `pickFirstResolvable` (single nearest visible ancestor, Phase 56) and `pickAllResolvable` (BFS traversal, diamond-hierarchy safe, Phase 56.1)
- `viewer-store.ts`: many-to-many fields (`focalNodeId`, `selectedNodeIds`, `selectedBucketEntityIds`, `selectedNodeTickKeys`, `selectedBucketKeys`); atomic action functions only; 0 direct `setState` calls; deep-equal guard on `setLslFilterEntityIds`
- `SidePanel.tsx`: `isSingleFocalMode` predicate — single-focal ticks bypass `BucketCardList` and render `EntityDetailPanel` directly (PATTERNS-LOCK Contract #3)
- `OntologyFilter` (Phase 60, 60-05 complete): API-driven path via `apiClient` prop; `listOntologyClasses` called at runtime; `CODING_SCHEMA` removed; `VOKB_SCHEMA` and L0/L1/L2 branching logic intact; 15/15 tests passing
- `FilterRail` (Phase 60): adapted to new `OntologyFilter` API contract; 16/16 tests passing; 2 auto-fixes applied to test suite

**Phase 56 (complete):** viewer-store slice, D3GraphCanvas audit, `pickFirstResolvable`; 6 locked state-model contracts in `56-PATTERNS.md`; AC #3 retraction locked in `56-CONTEXT.md`.

**Phase 56.1 (complete):** many-to-many store refactor; `pickAllResolvable` BFS; two-tier halo + drill-collapse; SidePanel mode-switch; E2E specs + PATTERNS-LOCK. Deferred: selection-history stack, Esc-key routing, viewport-fit transitions, 2 `toggleLayer` failures (tracked in `deferred-items.md`).

**Phase 60 (Wave 1 in progress):** 6 plans across 3 waves; `research_enabled: false`. Plans 60-04 and 60-06 require operator approval (`autonomous: false`). 60-04 pinned to main-tree for submodule context (`lib/km-core`, `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis`). 60-05 done; 60-04 blocked at human-verify checkpoint; worktree merge on hold pending 60-01 and 60-04.

**Phase 60.1 (scoped, pending implementation):** Four UX integrity issues documented as a follow-up scope:
1. **Sidebar item count mismatch**: `showDebugEntityTypes` toggle hides `Observation`/`Digest` entity types from graph rendering but not from the sidebar header count (e.g., 27 total vs. 10 visible), causing divergence from graph halos. Fix: two-line sidebar header with visible/hidden breakdown and toggle hint.
2. **Missing bidirectional hover**: both signal paths are unimplemented — graph→store→sidebar highlight path, and sidebar→store→D3 pulse path; entities filtered out of the current view need a visual indicator when hovered from the sidebar.
3. **SC#5 routing gap** (pre-existing, scoped alongside 60.1 issues)
4. **Legend-shape mismatch** (pre-existing, scoped alongside 60.1 issues)

## Key Files
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/components/D3GraphCanvas.tsx` — two-tier halo; drill-collapse; `focalNodeId`/`selectedNodeIds`; `nodeToBuckets` via `useRef`
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/panels/coding/LslTimelineStrip.tsx` — `lslFilterEntityIds` union includes halo ancestors; `pickAllResolvable`; zero direct `setState`
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/panels/coding/LslTimelineStrip.test.tsx` — 38 unit tests; T37 halo-union regression
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/panels/SidePanel.tsx` — `isSingleFocalMode` predicate; single-focal bypass to `EntityDetailPanel`
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/panels/SidePanel.test.tsx` — 13 vitest tests covering `isSingleFocalMode` branches
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/graph/ancestry.ts` — `pickFirstResolvable` and `pickAllResolvable` (BFS, diamond-safe)
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/graph/D3GraphCanvas.test.ts` — 21 unit graph tests; G21 stale-closure regression
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/store/viewer-store.ts` — Phase-56 + Phase-56.1 store slice; all many-to-many fields; atomic actions; deep-equal guard
- `tests/e2e/unified-viewer/56-bidirectional-selection.spec.ts` — 4 Playwright E2E tests
- `tests/e2e/unified-viewer/56.1-many-to-many.spec.ts` — 7 Playwright E2E tests (AC#1–AC#5, AC#8–AC#9)
- `.planning/phases/56-unified-viewer-bidirectional-selection-timeline-scale/56-PATTERNS.md` — 6 locked state-model contracts
- `.planning/phases/56.1-unified-viewer-many-to-many-bridge/56.1-PATTERNS-LOCK.md` — 7 Phase-56.1 durable contracts
- `.planning/phases/56.1-unified-viewer-many-to-many-bridge/deferred-items.md` — deferred items log (2 `toggleLayer` failures)
- `.planning/phases/60-unified-viewer-rendering-ux-integrity/` — Phase 60 planning directory; `60-CONTEXT.md`, plan files 60-01 through 60-06
- `.planning/todos/pending/2026-06-17-ontologyfilter-runtime-routing-gap.md` — Phase 60.1 scope document; sidebar count mismatch, bidirectional hover, SC#5 routing gap, legend-shape mismatch all specified here

## Usage
VOKB at `localhost:3002`; VKB at `localhost:8080`; unified viewer at `localhost:5173/viewer/coding` (port 5174 when 5173 is occupied by another worktree).

Phase 56: tick click → `pickFirstResolvable` → D3 ring + trace; graph node click → direct structural ID → tick highlights.

Phase 56.1: `pickAllResolvable` maps tick entity IDs to all visible structural ancestors; `focalNodeId` = primary; `selectedNodeIds` = co-selected; two-tier halo visually distinguishes them; halo ancestors kept mounted via `lslFilterEntityIds` union; `nodeToBuckets` lights all touching ticks on graph click; single-focal ticks bypass `BucketCardList` via `isSingleFocalMode`.

Phase 60: `OntologyFilter` fetches classes via `listOntologyClasses` through the wired `apiClient` prop at runtime (no static `CODING_SCHEMA`); `FilterRail` consumes the new API contract.

Phase 60.1: sidebar count mismatch and bidirectional hover are fully specified in `.planning/todos/pending/2026-06-17-ontologyfilter-runtime-routing-gap.md`; implementation not yet started.

## Troubleshooting
- **OntologyFilter renders static schema instead of live data**: verify `apiClient` prop is passed and `listOntologyClasses` is callable; `CODING_SCHEMA` must be absent — its removal is intentional
- **FilterRail tests fail after Phase 60 OntologyFilter changes**: test contract changed; consult Phase 60 plan 60-05 summary for the exact adapter fixes applied
- **Phase 60 worktree merge blocked**: held until plans 60-01 and 60-04 complete; 60-04 requires operator approval at its human-verify checkpoint
- **Sidebar item count diverges from visible graph nodes**: `showDebugEntityTypes` toggle excludes `Observation`/`Digest` from graph rendering but not from sidebar header count — this is the Phase 60.1 mismatch; two-line header fix is specified in `2026-06-17-ontologyfilter-runtime-routing-gap.md`
- **Bidirectional hover non-functional (graph↔sidebar)**: both signal paths (graph→store→sidebar highlight, sidebar→store→D3 pulse) are unimplemented; tracked as a Phase 60.1 issue with full spec in the 60.1 planning file
- **Halo nodes disappear on tick filter change**: resolved ancestors not in `lslFilterEntityIds` union — verify `onTickClick` unions them before store write
- **Graph→Timeline tick highlighting stale**: `nodeToBuckets` in stale closure — use `useRef` read pattern at click time
- **Single-focal tick shows `BucketCardList`**: `isSingleFocalMode` not evaluating correctly; requires exactly 1 resolved node
- **Phase 56.1 tick illuminates only one graph node**: `pickFirstResolvable` in use instead of `pickAllResolvable`
- **Two-tier halo not distinguishing focal from co-selected**: verify `focalNodeId` and `selectedNodeIds` populated atomically in the store action
- **`toggleLayer` test failures (2)**: pre-existing deferred failures; tracked in `deferred-items.md`; not regressions
- **Spurious D3 rebuilds on filter change**: deep-equal guard missing in `setLslFilterEntityIds`
- **`useViewerStore.setState` called directly**: violates Phase 56 audit contract; replace with atomic store action functions
- **Cross-agent worktree conflict**: each wave uses a dedicated isolated worktree; verify per-wave isolation before dispatch
