- **Stage/chunk composite math**: correct progress display requires combining stage index and within-stage chunk ratio rather than using the raw chunk ratio alone

## Key Files
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/components/consolidation-progress.tsx` — shared progress bar component; chunk ratio calculation
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/server.js` — forwards `lastStderrAt` and computes `stderrAgeMs` in the inflight status object
- `~/Agentic/coding/src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js` — emits per-stage progress events; heartbeat records `lastStderrAt`; implements per-project root resolution logic to correctly map observations to their respective project roots during consolidation

## Usage
Rendered on the digests and insights dashboard pages whenever a consolidation run is in-flight. The component reads `startedAt`, `ageMs`, `lastMessage`, `stderrAgeMs`, and N/M stage parsing from the heartbeat JSON. After changes to `ObservationConsolidator.js`, restart obs-api to deploy the updated consolidation logic.

## Troubleshooting
- **Progress bar jumps to 100% prematurely**: chunk ratio calculation error in `consolidation-progress.tsx` — ratio should reflect end-of-chunk, not start; verify composite stage/chunk math
- **Progress bar freezes during Stage 2**: Stage 2 `"Insights updated: N"` messages match no regex — extend the parser to recognize these terminal summary lines
- **Staleness detection fires too early**: heartbeat timestamps are wall-clock bumped rather than stderr-activity bumped — verify `lastStderrAt` is being recorded in `consolidate-observations.js` and forwarded by `server.js`
- **Button stuck in "consolidating" after run completes**: heartbeat polling should detect completion; if port 3033 is unreachable the consolidator process may have hung — check for unclosed connections
metadata.scope: Live consolidation run progress display, elapsed time tracking, and stuck-process detection within the system health dashboard.

topic: MCP Constraint Monitor Dashboard — Port 3030 and Service Configuration
summary:
## Purpose
Provides a web UI for the MCP constraint monitor, served on port 3030. The backend API runs separately on port 3031 via `dashboard-server.js`.

## Architecture
- **Port 3030**: Should be served by the Next.js app at `integrations/mcp-constraint-monitor/dashboard/`; mapped in `docker-compose` but requires an explicit `supervisord` entry to start it
- **Port 3031**: The API server (`dashboard-server.js`) — this is the only process `supervisord` starts by default; 3030 goes unserved unless the Next.js process is also added
- **Port governance**: All port values must come exclusively from the shared `.env.ports` file; no port values may be hardcoded in `package.json`, `supervisord.conf`, or invented ad-hoc in `.env`

## Key Files
- `integrations/mcp-constraint-monitor/dashboard/` — Next.js app that should serve on port 3030
- `supervisord.conf` — must include a process entry for the Next.js app to activate port 3030
- `Dockerfile.coding-services` — Docker build for the services container
- `~/Agentic/_work/rapid-automations/.env.ports` — single source of truth for all port assignments
- `temporal.ts`, `routes.ts` — API routing files edited during port debugging

## Usage
- Access the dashboard UI at port 3030; the REST API is at port 3031
- After any `supervisord.conf` change, rebuild/restart the container for changes to take effect
- Port values must be read from `.env.ports` by all consumers

## Troubleshooting
- **Port 3030 returns connection refused**: `supervisord` is not starting the Next.js app — add a `[program:dashboard-ui]` entry pointing to `npm start` (or equivalent) in `supervisord.conf` and rebuild the container
- **Port remapping after docker-compose restart**: Docker may re-map ports unexpectedly; verify the `docker-compose.yml` port mapping and that the internal process is actually listening
- **Unauthorized port entries in `.env`**: Remove any port values not sourced from `.env.ports`; the canonical `.env` must not contain invented `*_PORT` entries
- **UI fix not visible after deployment**: Check for browser caching first — hard refresh with `Cmd+Shift+R` before investigating the bundle

metadata.topic: MCP Constraint Monitor Dashboard — Port 3030 and Service Configuration

topic: VS Code SCM Workspace Configuration — Multi-Repo Visibility
summary:
## Purpose
Ensures the main `coding` repo, `.specstory/history` private repo, and all `integrations/` sub-repos are simultaneously visible in VS Code's Source Control panel without triggering enterprise auth failures or repo-list teardowns.

## Architecture
- **Root cause of disappearing parent repo**: The nested `.specstory/history/.git` repo (pointing at a GHE remote) triggers enterprise auth failures that cause VS Code's git extension to tear down the entire repo list when auto-discovery traverses into `.specstory/`
- **Fix**: Explicitly declare all repos as named `folders` entries in `coding.code-workspace` rather than relying on auto-discovery; disable auto-detection in workspace settings
- **Suppression settings** (retain as safety net): `git.repositoryScanIgnoredFolders: [".specstory"]`, `git.openRepositoryInParentFolders: "never"`, `git.repositoryScanMaxDepth: 0`
- **Secondary bug**: VSCode's submodule auto-detection cascade overrides explicit workspace folder registrations — disabled by workspace setting
- **Final state**: `coding.code-workspace` lists all 8 repos explicitly: `coding`, `.specstory/history`, and 6 `integrations/` sub-repos; SCM panel set to parallel display mode

## Key Files
- `coding.code-workspace` — workspace definition; must list all 8 repos as explicit folder entries with auto-discovery disabled

## Usage
Opened as a workspace (`code coding.code-workspace`) rather than a plain folder. All git operations across all 8 repos are accessible from the SCM panel simultaneously.

## Troubleshooting
- **Parent repo disappears from SCM**: Auto-discovery is traversing into `.specstory/` and hitting GHE auth failures — verify `repositoryScanIgnoredFolders` includes `.specstory` and all repos are listed explicitly in `folders`
- **Sub-repo missing from SCM panel**: Not listed in `coding.code-workspace` `folders` entries — add it explicitly
- **Submodule auto-detection overriding explicit folders**: Disable submodule auto-detection in workspace settings
- **SCM shows duplicate entries**: Both auto-discovery and explicit folder entries are active — confirm auto-discovery is fully disabled

metadata.topic: VS Code SCM Workspace Configuration — Multi-Repo Visibility

topic: LLM Token Usage Tracking — Proxy Instrumentation and Dashboard
summary:
## Purpose
Tracks the completed extraction of the old `lib/llm/` library (~3,582 lines, 29 files) into the external `@rapid/llm-proxy` package, documents outstanding documentation debt, and notes which subsystems deliberately bypass the proxy.

## Architecture
- **Source migration**: complete — `lib/llm/` deleted in commit `cf983e5f`; all 6 TypeScript source files correctly import from `@rapid/llm-proxy` v1.0.0
- **External package location**: `<GHE>/adpnext-apps/rapid-llm-proxy`
- **`UnifiedInferenceEngine`**: survives only as a type interface in the ontology layer; standalone multi-provider routing now handled by `@rapid/llm-proxy`'s `LLMService`
- **Classification subsystem intentional bypass**: `SemanticAnalyzer.js` (Layer 4 of LSL classification) makes direct HTTP `fetch` calls to XAI/OpenAI — zero `@rapid/llm-proxy` or `lib/llm` references; this is deliberate, not an oversight

**Outstanding documentation debt (not yet remediated):**
- `docs/subscription-providers-implementation.md` — stale `lib/llm/` path references at lines 128, 133, 209–216
- `mkdocs.yml` — 3 broken nav entries: `architecture/llm-architecture.md`, `integrations/llm-cli-proxy.md`, `guides/llm-providers.md`
- `docs/knowledge-management/README.md` — references non-existent `src/inference/UnifiedInferenceEngine.js`
- PlantUML `.puml` sources are mostly current but exported PNGs may be stale

## Key Files
- `lib/llm/` — **deleted**; all references to this path are stale
- `@rapid/llm-proxy` — replacement package; imported in 6 source files
- `docs/subscription-providers-implementation.md` — stale references at lines 128, 133, 209–216
- `mkdocs.yml` — 3 broken nav entries for LLM architecture docs
- `src/live-logging/SemanticAnalyzer.js` — intentional direct HTTP bypass; no proxy usage

## Usage
All new LLM integration code should import from `@rapid/llm-proxy`. Do not re-introduce `lib/llm/` references. When editing docs, check that `lib/llm/` path references are updated to reflect the proxy package.

## Troubleshooting
- **Import error for `lib/llm/` path**: Library deleted; update to `@rapid/llm-proxy` import
- **`mkdocs build --strict` fails on LLM architecture pages**: 3 nav entries point to deleted files — remove from `mkdocs.yml` or create stub pages
- **`UnifiedInferenceEngine` described as standalone in docs**: Outdated — routing logic now lives in the proxy's `LLMService`; update documentation
- **LSL classification not using proxy**: Intentional design — `SemanticAnalyzer.js` bypasses the proxy via direct HTTP fetch

topic: Enhanced Transcript Monitor (ETM)
summary:
## Purpose
Captures, classifies, and archives live coding session transcripts into structured markdown files, enabling context reload across sessions and feeding the ETM observation pipeline. Acts as the primary source of truth for session history.

## Architecture
- **Command entry point**: `.claude/commands/sl.md` — triggers session log loading
- **24 core modules** in `src/live-logging/`: `StreamingTranscriptReader`, `ReliableCodingClassifier`, `LSLFileManager`, `ConfigurableRedactor`, `TranscriptNormalizer`, and others covering streaming, classification, routing, analysis, and file management
- **14 orchestration scripts**:
  - `enhanced-transcript-monitor.js` — primary runtime daemon
  - `global-lsl-coordinator.js` — multi-project coordination
  - `batch-lsl-processor.js` — offline/backfill processing
- **5-layer classification pipeline** in `ReliableCodingClassifier.js`: conversation bias → path analysis → keyword matching → embedding similarity → semantic LLM analysis; output routes to `CODING_INFRASTRUCTURE` or `NOT_CODING_INFRASTRUCTURE`
- **Supporting classifiers**: `PathAnalyzer`, `KeywordMatcher`, `ConversationBiasTracker`, `EmbeddingClassifier`, `SemanticAnalyzer`
- **LLM calls in classification (Layer 4)**: `SemanticAnalyzer.js` makes direct HTTP `fetch` calls to XAI/OpenAI APIs with a 2-second timeout — **not** routed through `@rapid/llm-proxy`; confirmed zero proxy references in this subsystem
- **`TranscriptNormalizer.js`** — normalizes transcript segments before downstream processing; `splitSpecstorySegments` detects and processes both legacy specstory segment formats and **post-ETM LSL tranche records** (multi-kilobyte structured JSON tranche data); companion functions `deterministicId` (stable ID generation for tranche records) and `offsetTimestamp` (time-alignment) live in the same file
- **Storage**: `.specstory/history/` (active), `.specstory/archive/` (rotated), `.specstory/trajectory/` (trajectory tracking)
- **Filename convention**: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM-HHMM_<userhash>[_from-<project>].md` — generated by `timezone-utils.js`; files over 200KB split at `## Prompt Set` boundaries with part numbers
- **Max file size**: 50 MB with automatic rotation to `.specstory/archive/`
- **Real-time trajectory tracking**: `TrajectoryHistoryService`
- **9 configuration files** governing LLM settings and redaction patterns

## Key Files
- `.claude/commands/sl.md` — session log load command
- `src/live-logging/ReliableCodingClassifier.js` — 5-layer classification engine
- `src/live-logging/SemanticAnalyzer.js` — Layer 4 LLM calls; direct HTTP fetch to XAI/OpenAI; 2s timeout
- `src/live-logging/TranscriptNormalizer.js` — `splitSpecstorySegments` (handles legacy + post-ETM LSL tranche format); `deterministicId`; `offsetTimestamp`
- `src/live-logging/enhanced-transcript-monitor.js` — primary orchestration runtime; calls `classify()` at line 2657
- `src/live-logging/LSLFileManager.js` — file writing, rotation, naming
- `src/live-logging/ConfigurableRedactor.js` — redaction before write
- `scripts/global-lsl-coordinator.js` — multi-project coordination
- `scripts/batch-lsl-processor.js` — offline processing
- `scripts/timezone-utils.js` — `getTimeWindow()`, filename generation

## Usage
ETM daemon watches transcripts continuously. Classification routes exchanges to project-specific LSL directories. `/sl` command loads history at session start. `TranscriptNormalizer.splitSpecstorySegments` is invoked within the specstory pipeline to split and identify observation segments; it must handle both the original format and the post-ETM LSL tranche format (structured JSON tranche records). Integration points: `CLAUDE.md`, `copilot-instructions.md`, `mkdocs.yml`.

## Troubleshooting
- **Session not classified to correct project**: Check `ReliableCodingClassifier.js` layer cascade — each layer must pass or fall through; semantic LLM layer (Layer 4) uses direct HTTP, not the proxy
- **Files not rotating**: Verify 50 MB threshold in `LSLFileManager.js`; archive dir must exist and be gitignored
- **Context missing after session reload**: Files may be split across numbered parts — load all parts for the date range
- **SemanticAnalyzer failing**: Direct HTTP fetch to XAI/OpenAI with 2-second timeout — check API key env vars; not routed through LLM proxy so proxy health is irrelevant
- **LSL tranche records not detected in specstory pipeline**: `splitSpecstorySegments` in `TranscriptNormalizer.js` must handle both legacy segment format and post-ETM LSL tranche records; if tranche data is silently dropped, verify the tranche detection branch is present and that `deterministicId`/`offsetTimestamp` are invoked correctly on tranche payloads
- **Specstory backfill gap recovery producing missing observations**: LSL tranche parsing failure in `TranscriptNormalizer.js` is a known root cause — confirm tranche format support is deployed before re-running backfill

metadata.topic: Enhanced Transcript Monitor (ETM)

topic: Knowledge Context Injection — Embedding Pipeline
summary:
## Purpose
Provides semantic vector search over the project's accumulated observations, digests, insights, and KG entities to enable context injection into agent prompts. Backs the standalone retrieval HTTP service (port 3848) consumed by all agent adapter layers.

## Architecture
- **Embedding service** (`src/embedding-service.ts`): uses `fastembed` for local, platform-native text embedding
- **fastembed cache**: pre-warmed during the Docker **builder stage** and baked into the production image (~90MB); the `local_cache` directory is **not** bind-mounted at runtime — cache must exist in the image or the process fails with `FATAL` at startup
- **Qdrant**: stores vectors in 4 collections: `observations`, `digests`, `insights`, `kg_entities`
- **SHA-256 content hashing** (`src/content-hash.ts`): provides idempotency — already-embedded items are skipped on re-run
- **Write-time hook**: a Redis pub/sub listener (`embedding-listener` supervisord process) auto-embeds new observations as they are written
- **Proxy env vars**: `supervisord.conf` inherits proxy environment variables (with `NO_PROXY` covering internal services) rather than clearing them

## Key Files
- `src/embedding-service.ts` — core embedding logic, fastembed integration
- `src/content-hash.ts` — SHA-256 idempotency hashing
- `src/qdrant-collections.ts` — Qdrant collection schema definitions
- `embedding-config.json` — embedding model and collection configuration (path must be relative to compiled output location)
- `docker/Dockerfile.coding-services` — builder stage pre-warms fastembed cache; copies to production image
- `docker/supervisord.conf` — starts `embedding-listener`; inherits proxy env vars

## Usage
The backfill CLI is run once (or after bulk data imports). The `embedding-listener` Redis pub/sub daemon runs as a background supervisord process, embedding new observations automatically. The retrieval service on port 3848 is called by agent hook adapters.

## Troubleshooting
- **`embedding-listener` shows `FATAL` / "Exited too quickly"**: fastembed local cache is missing from the image — rebuild `Dockerfile.coding-services` so the builder stage pre-warms and copies the cache (~90MB)
- **External fetch fails from inside container**: verify `supervisord.conf` inherits `http_proxy`/`https_proxy` and sets `NO_PROXY` for internal hosts
- **`embedding-config.json` not found at runtime**: `__dirname` in compiled output resolves to `dist/embedding/` — use `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../embedding-config.json')` or an env var absolute path
- **New observations not appearing in Qdrant**: verify `embedding-listener` is `RUNNING` via supervisorctl; check Redis pub/sub publication; manually trigger backfill CLI for missed items
- **fastembed fails to load tokenizer binaries**: `--ignore-scripts` npm install flag blocks postinstall scripts — use a separate install step without that flag for fastembed

topic: LSL (Live Session Logging) Infrastructure
summary:
## Purpose
LSL captures LLM coding session transcripts into per-hour markdown files in `.specstory/history/`, providing session continuity across agent restarts and a human-readable audit trail. It is the event-capture layer of the broader observations pipeline.

## Architecture
- **Session log location**: `.specstory/history/` — markdown files named by time window (e.g., `1100-1200.md`)
- **ETM daemon**: `~/Agentic/coding/enhanced-transcript-monitor.js` — managed by launchd; writes LSL files as transcript events arrive; its health is independently verifiable (launchd status + LSL file recency)
- **Global LSL Coordinator**: manages ETM lifecycle; registers ETM with PSM; kills ETM on failed tmux session liveness checks
- **Log flow**: ETM transcript extraction → exchange classification → LSL markdown write to `.specstory/history/`
- **Health indicator**: `[LSL●]` in tmux status bar — driven by `combined-status-line-wrapper.js` → `getLSLHealthStatus()` → `health-prompt-hook.js`
- **False-positive mitigation**: ETM `coding-keywords.json` exclusion patterns prevent non-coding content from being written to the coding project's history
- **Startup ritual**: `/sl` skill command reads recent history files to restore session context at agent launch

## Key Files
- `~/Agentic/coding/enhanced-transcript-monitor.js` — ETM daemon; writes LSL files
- `combined-status-line-wrapper.js` — tmux status line; contains `getLSLHealthStatus()`
- `health-prompt-hook.js` — writes health file read by the status line
- `.specstory/history/` — output directory for session log markdown files

## Usage
The Global LSL Coordinator auto-starts the ETM. The `/sl` skill is invoked at session start to load prior context. LSL files feed the observations pipeline (obs-api → observations database) and are also used for direct context recovery. Note: ETM health (LSL file writing) is independent of obs-api health — ETM can be healthy while the downstream observations pipeline is stalled.

## Troubleshooting
- **Red `[LSL●]` indicator**: trace through `combined-status-line-wrapper.js` → `getLSLHealthStatus()` → `health-prompt-hook.js`; check health file content for the specific failing condition
- **Missing LSL window files**: check whether ETM was killed by the coordinator during an IDE session gap; also check `coding/.specstory/history/` for misdirected files caused by false-positive classification
- **ETM healthy but no new observations in database**: stall is downstream of ETM in obs-api or the observations database — see Observations Pipeline troubleshooting
- **Assistant not reading history at startup**: ensure `/sl` is invoked as the first action of each session

- **Missing LSL window files**: check whether ETM was killed by the coordinator during an IDE session gap; also check `coding/.specstory/history/` for misdirected files caused by false-positive classification
- **ETM healthy but no new observations in database**: stall is downstream of ETM in obs-api or the observations database — see Observations Pipeline troubleshooting
- **Assistant not reading history at startup**: ensure `/sl` is invoked as the first action of each session
metadata.scope: The ETM daemon, file-write pipeline, and tmux health indicator that capture and surface LLM session transcripts as LSL markdown files.

topic: Knowledge Context Injection — Embedding Pipeline
summary:
## Purpose
Provides semantic vector search over the project's accumulated observations, digests, insights, and KG entities to enable context injection into agent prompts. Backs the standalone retrieval HTTP service (port 3848) consumed by all agent adapter layers.

## Architecture
- **Embedding service** (`src/embedding-service.ts`): uses `fastembed` for local, platform-native text embedding
- **fastembed cache**: pre-warmed during the Docker **builder stage** and baked into the production image (~90MB); the `local_cache` directory is **not** bind-mounted at runtime — cache must exist in the image or the process fails with `FATAL` at startup
- **Qdrant**: stores vectors in 4 collections: `observations`, `digests`, `insights`, `kg_entities`
- **SHA-256 content hashing** (`src/content-hash.ts`): provides idempotency — already-embedded items are skipped on re-run
- **Write-time hook**: a Redis pub/sub listener (`embedding-listener` supervisord process) auto-embeds new observations as they are written
- **Proxy env vars**: `supervisord.conf` inherits proxy environment variables (with `NO_PROXY` covering internal services) rather than clearing them

## Key Files
- `src/embedding-service.ts` — core embedding logic, fastembed integration
- `src/content-hash.ts` — SHA-256 idempotency hashing
- `src/qdrant-collections.ts` — Qdrant collection schema definitions
- `embedding-config.json` — embedding model and collection configuration (path must be relative to compiled output location)
- `docker/Dockerfile.coding-services` — builder stage pre-warms fastembed cache; copies to production image
- `docker/supervisord.conf` — starts `embedding-listener`; inherits proxy env vars

## Usage
The backfill CLI is run once (or after bulk data imports). The `embedding-listener` Redis pub/sub daemon runs as a background supervisord process, embedding new observations automatically. The retrieval service on port 3848 is called by agent hook adapters.

## Troubleshooting
- **`embedding-listener` shows `FATAL` / "Exited too <USER_ID_REDACTED>"**: fastembed local cache is missing from the image — rebuild `Dockerfile.coding-services` so the builder stage pre-warms and copies the cache (~90MB)
- **External fetch fails from inside container**: verify `supervisord.conf` inherits `http_proxy`/`https_proxy` and sets `NO_PROXY` for internal hosts
- **`embedding-config.json` not found at runtime**: `__dirname` in compiled output resolves to `dist/embedding/` — use `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../embedding-config.json')` or an env var absolute path
- **New observations not appearing in Qdrant**: verify `embedding-listener` is `RUNNING` via supervisorctl; check Redis pub/sub publication; manually trigger backfill CLI for missed items
- **fastembed fails to load tokenizer binaries**: `--ignore-scripts` npm install flag blocks postinstall scripts — use a separate install step without that flag for fastembed

topic: Observations Database and Backfill Pipeline
summary:
## Purpose
Two complementary mechanisms write to and correct the observations database: (1) targeted gap-window backfill scripts that recover specific missing observations from identified empty time ranges in the transcript timeline, and (2) a planned async post-write resolver (Phase 50) that corrects pronoun antecedents and recovers image-stripped rows after the primary write path. Both share a core constraint: writes must be strictly bounded to confirmed-empty windows to prevent duplicates.

## Architecture
**Gap-window targeted backfill:**
- `backfill-gap-observations.mjs` recovers missing observations from a confirmed-empty transcript time range
- Pre-write gate: a read-only diagnostic scan confirms no rows exist in the target window before any writes proceed; dry-run validation is mandatory before live execution
- Posts via `/api/observations/messages` endpoint; preserves original timestamps
- Uses a session-aware hash function (`sessionId`-keyed) to guarantee dedup safety against the existing corpus
- `ObservationWriter.init()` must be called before the write loop; skipping it bypasses dedup and initialization

**Session-hash collision risk:**
- After a session-hash function change, bulk reprocessing of already-stored rows generates fresh hashes that collide with existing records, producing duplicates
- Safe pattern: constrain backfill exclusively to confirmed-empty windows; never bulk-reprocess the full existing corpus after a hash function change

**Phase 50 async resolver (planned):**
- Background job resolving pronoun antecedents and recovering image-only rows after `ObservationWriter` writes
- Context window scoped to active user interaction time (not wall-clock elapsed); long-running autonomous tasks use a fixed last-N-exchanges fallback
- Reads verbatim LSL transcript files for context unavailable at write time
- Subsumes Phase 47's historical image-stripped row recovery scope

**Semantic dedup (synchronous):** duplicate observations are silently dropped at write time; drop count is reported in run output but no rows are stored.

## Key Files
- `backfill-gap-observations.mjs` — targeted gap-window backfill script; dry-run-validate before live execution
- `src/live-logging/ObservationWriter.js` — primary write handler; `init()` required before any backfill script's write loop; contains semantic dedup logic
- `/api/observations/messages` — HTTP endpoint for posting observation records (used by gap-window backfill)
- `.planning/phases/50-CONTEXT.md` — full design specification for the Phase 50 async resolver
- `.planning/phases/47-CONTEXT.md` — Phase 47 scope boundary (image-stripped row recovery belongs to Phase 50)
- `.planning/phases/STATE.md` — current implementation state

## Usage
**Gap-window backfill:** (1) run read-only diagnostic scan to confirm target window contains no rows; (2) dry-run validate the script; (3) execute live only after both checks pass. Never apply to windows that already contain observations.

**Phase 50 resolver:** not yet implemented; once active, runs as a background job scanning for unresolved-reference and image-only observations, re-processing them using verbatim LSL transcripts.

## Troubleshooting
- **Duplicate rows after backfill**: session-hash change + broad reprocessing generates new hashes for existing rows — constrain writes strictly to confirmed-empty time windows
- **`ObservationWriter.init()` not called before write loop**: required by backfill scripts; skipping causes dedup failures — verify `init()` is called first
- **Unexpected duplicate rows not being deduped**: confirm `ObservationWriter.init()` was called before the write loop began
- **First-in-session pronoun loss not resolved**: expected until Phase 50 is implemented; record affected row IDs for later re-processing
- **Image-only rows not recovered**: unrecoverable from DB alone until Phase 50 reads verbatim transcripts; do not attempt manual LLM backfill (no summarizable source text)
- **Context window boundary incorrect in Phase 50 design**: use active user interaction time, not wall-clock elapsed; use fixed last-N-exchanges fallback for autonomous long-running tasks

topic: obs-api Ontology Registry Sourcing Architecture
summary:
## Purpose
Documents the architectural split between obs-api's bundled ontology registry and the host-side ontology directory. L0 anchor classes (`System`, `Project`) are synthesized correctly by the km-core API handler at port 12436. L1/L2 class annotations added to `.data/ontologies/` remain invisible to `GraphKMStore`-backed <USER_ID_REDACTED> and the unified viewer filter until a writer-validation-gated `ontologyDir` reroute is implemented (Phase 60.08/61, Deviation 3).

## Architecture
- **Default registry load path**: `GraphKMStore` reads ontology class definitions from the **bundled** `lib/km-core/ontology/` directory at startup
- **Host-side directory ignored**: `.data/ontologies/` is not consulted; L1/L2 classes annotated there are absent from all `GraphKMStore`-backed <USER_ID_REDACTED> and viewer filter group headers
- **Bundled-only classes**: `LearningArtifact`, `Digest`, `Insight`, `Observation` exist in the bundled registry but are absent from host `upper.json`; writer-side validation for these classes depends entirely on the bundled path remaining active
- **L0 synthesis unaffected**: the km-core API handler at port 12436 synthesizes `System` and `Project` L0 anchor classes correctly via `HIERARCHY_ROOTS` + `extends` fallback — this path bypasses the registry sourcing gap
- **L1/L2 blocked (expected)**: L1/L2 group headers absent from the viewer filter is expected behavior under the bundled-registry constraint, not a handler defect
- **Fix location**: `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs` line 1336 — a single `ontologyDir` swap reroutes `GraphKMStore` to `.data/ontologies/`
- **Architectural gate**: the reroute requires a writer-validation audit confirming the four bundled-only classes are added to host `upper.json` before the swap; applying the one-liner without this audit orphans writer-side validation
- **Decision classification**: Rule-4 cross-cutting architectural decision; deferred to Phase 60.08 or 61; Deviation 3 in `60-VERIFICATION.md` with status `PARTIAL (L0 lit; L1/L2 blocked)`

## Key Files
- `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs` — line 1336: `ontologyDir` configuration for `GraphKMStore`; the pending fix target
- `lib/km-core/ontology/` — bundled ontology registry; loaded by default; contains `LearningArtifact`, `Digest`, `Insight`, `Observation`
- `.data/ontologies/` — host-side ontology directory; contains newly annotated L1/L2 classes; not read by current obs-api
- `.data/ontologies/upper.json` — host-side L0/L1/L2 hierarchy; does not yet contain the four bundled-only classes
- `.planning/phases/60-unified-viewer-rendering-ux-integrity/60-VERIFICATION.md` — Deviation 3 record; status `PARTIAL (L0 lit; L1/L2 blocked)`
- `.planning/todos/pending/2026-06-17-ontologyfilter-runtime-routing-gap.md` — follow-up phase draft for the reroute and writer-validation audit

## Usage
The bundled-registry behavior is intentional for writer-side class validation. L0 anchor classes are correctly synthesized by the km-core API handler. L1/L2 classes added to `.data/ontologies/` will not appear in viewer filters or `GraphKMStore` results until Phase 60.08/61 implements the reroute with an accompanying writer-validation audit. Do not apply the one-liner fix without first completing the audit and adding bundled-only classes to host `upper.json`.

## Troubleshooting
- **L1/L2 group headers absent from viewer filter**: expected; Deviation 3 is deferred to Phase 60.08/61; not a handler defect
- **L0 group headers (`System`/`Project`) absent from viewer filter**: unexpected; verify km-core API handler at port 12436 is running and `HIERARCHY_ROOTS` registry is compiled
- **`LearningArtifact`/`Digest`/`Insight`/`Observation` validation breaks after reroute**: these are absent from host `upper.json`; add them before or alongside the `ontologyDir` swap
- **Confirming which ontology path is active**: inspect line 1336 of `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs`; `lib/km-core/ontology/` = bundled (current default), `.data/ontologies/` = host (pending)
- **Deviation 3 re-evaluated as blocking**: operator options — (a) partial-PASS + Phase 60.08/61 follow-up [recommended], (b) inline fix with full writer-validation audit, (c) reject and triage

topic: LSL Session Continuity Bootstrap
summary:
## Purpose
LSL files are the primary verbatim record of coding sessions in the km-core project. Loading recent LSL files at the start of a new session is the standard procedure to re-establish task state, in-progress work, and likely next steps without relying on memory or external summaries.

## Architecture
- **File storage**: LSL files are dated flat files in `.specstory/history/`. Sub-agent logs follow `YYYY-MM-DD-HHHH-HHHH_S1_<index>-<ID-HASH>`; main session LSLs use analogous date-stamped naming.
- **Two-tier content**: each LSL contains a verbatim transcript tier (raw turn content) and may be accompanied by observation/summary records in the observations database.
- **Length-based detection threshold**: if the primary (most-recent) file is `< 50KB` or `< 500 lines`, additional historical files must be loaded; a single short file is never sufficient alone.
- **Aggregation minimum**: the four most recent files spanning the last 1–2 days is the recommended floor; individual sessions are typically too short for sufficient context in isolation.
- **Timestamp-based filtering**: applied to identify files within the relevant recent time window when the directory contains many candidates.
- **Cross-project context resolution**: the protocol includes a resolution step for environments where multiple projects share history infrastructure.
- **Load order**: oldest-to-newest — reversed order causes decisions to appear before their motivating context.

## Key Files
- `.specstory/history/` — active session log directory; all LSL markdown files for the current project
- `scripts/backfill-subagent-transcripts.mjs` — recovers sub-agent LSL content for gaps not captured live
- `.planning/phases/STATE.md` — complements LSL content with current phase implementation state
- `projects.json`, `defaultDir.ts` — used to resolve the exact `.specstory/history/` path per project

## Usage
Invoked at session start before any substantive work. Procedure: (1) query `.specstory/history/` directory for recent files; (2) apply length-based detection — if primary file is `< 50KB` or `< 500 lines`, pull additional historical files; (3) load files oldest-to-newest; (4) cross-reference `.planning/phases/STATE.md` for planning context. Recovery goals: which tasks were active, what state each was left in, what next steps are implied. Read-only — no artifacts are modified during bootstrap.

## Troubleshooting
- **Insufficient context from a single file**: apply the length threshold — `< 50KB` or `< 500 lines` requires loading additional files before concluding context is established
- **Bootstrap stalls mid-execution**: the directory-listing bash step may return slowly or hang; re-invoke the query before proceeding to the load steps
- **Narrative incoherence after loading**: verify oldest-to-newest load order; reversed order makes decisions appear before their antecedents
- **Task details not surfaced by LSL alone**: cross-reference `.planning/phases/STATE.md` and the relevant `*-CONTEXT.md` phase file for structured task state
- **Sub-agent turns missing from LSLs**: run `backfill-subagent-transcripts.mjs` with a 1-day recency filter before treating the LSL record as complete

summary:
## Purpose
Documents the architectural split between obs-api's bundled ontology registry and the host-side ontology directory, which causes newly annotated L1/L2 classes to be invisible to `GraphKMStore`-backed queries and the unified viewer filter. This is the root cause of Phase 60-07 Deviation 3 — a deferred cross-cutting architectural decision requiring operator judgment before it can be resolved.

## Architecture
- **Default registry load path**: `obs-api`'s `GraphKMStore` reads ontology class definitions from the **bundled** `lib/km-core/ontology/` directory at startup
- **Host-side directory ignored**: `.data/ontologies/` is not consulted; L1/L2 classes annotated or added there are absent from all `GraphKMStore`-backed queries and the viewer filter
- **Bundled-only classes**: `LearningArtifact`, `Digest`, `Insight`, `Observation` exist in the bundled registry but are **absent** from the host `upper.json`; writer-side validation for these classes depends entirely on the bundled path remaining active
- **Fix location**: `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs` line 1336 — a single `ontologyDir` swap reroutes `GraphKMStore` to `.data/ontologies/`

metadata.topic: obs-api Ontology Registry Sourcing Architecture
metadata.summary:
## Purpose
Documents the architectural split between obs-api's bundled ontology registry and the host-side ontology directory, which causes newly annotated L1/L2 classes to be invisible to `GraphKMStore`-backed queries and the unified viewer filter. This is the root cause of Phase 60-07 Deviation 3 — a deferred cross-cutting architectural decision requiring operator judgment before it can be resolved.

## Architecture
- **Default registry load path**: `obs-api`'s `GraphKMStore` reads ontology class definitions from the **bundled** `lib/km-core/ontology/` directory at startup

id: km-core Knowledge Graph Hydration — JSON Export Priority and Build Safety
topic: km-core Knowledge Graph Hydration — JSON Export Priority and Build Safety
summary:
## Purpose
The `hydrate()` function in `lib/km-core/src/store/persistence.ts` controls whether the in-memory knowledge graph loads from LevelDB cache or the canonical JSON export. Without a patch, `hydrate()` defaults to LevelDB, which may be stale — causing catastrophic graph collapse. The patch is build-fragile: `npm run build` regenerates the compiled dist artifact and discards any manual patch to that file, requiring both the TypeScript source and the compiled artifact to be patched independently after every build.

## Architecture
- **Unpatched behavior**: prefers LevelDB cache; stale cache produces collapse (observed: ~1270 nodes/1594 edges → 924 nodes/3 edges)
- **Patched behavior**: prefers `.data/exports/knowledge-export/general.json` as the authoritative load source
- **Build fragility**: `npm run build` compiles `persistence.ts` → `dist/store/persistence.js`; any manual patch to the dist file is overwritten; the source patch survives the build but the dist patch does not
- **Two required patch targets simultaneously**:
  - `lib/km-core/src/store/persistence.ts` — TypeScript source
  - `lib/km-core/dist/store/persistence.js` — compiled artifact; must be re-patched after every `npm run build`
- **Recovery sequence**: (1) re-apply patch to both files, (2) wipe stale LevelDB, (3) rehydrate from `general.json`, (4) restart `obs-api` and VKB
- **Healthy post-recovery graph state**: 816 nodes / 1262 edges; edge type distribution: `contains: 849`, `related_to: 202`, `capturedBy: 112`, `has_insight: 67`, `parent-child: 11`
- **Open writer-side issue**: `ObservationConsolidator`/`ObservationWriter` still actively emit `Observation` and `Digest` entities to km-core post-architectural-removal; filter these from the JSON export before loading until the writer-side fix lands

## Key Files
- `lib/km-core/src/store/persistence.ts` — `hydrate()` source; patch to prefer JSON over LevelDB
- `lib/km-core/dist/store/persistence.js` — compiled artifact; must also be patched after every `npm run build`
- `.data/exports/knowledge-export/general.json` — canonical JSON export; authoritative rehydration source

## Usage
After every `npm run build` in the km-core workspace, verify that `dist/store/persistence.js` still contains the JSON-preference patch before restarting `obs-api` or VKB. The patch has not been absorbed into the build process — manual post-build verification and re-patching of the dist file is required on every build. All Insights should be tagged `source='auto'` after a clean rehydration to clear any false "Manual" badges.

## Troubleshooting
- **Graph collapses after a build (nodes/edges drop dramatically)**: `npm run build` overwrote the dist patch; re-apply to `lib/km-core/dist/store/persistence.js`, wipe LevelDB, rehydrate from `general.json`, restart `obs-api` and VKB
- **Source patched but graph still collapses**: patching `persistence.ts` alone is insufficient if the dist was compiled before the patch — always patch `dist/store/persistence.js` directly regardless of source state
- **After rehydration, spurious `Observation`/`Digest` entities appear**: `ObservationConsolidator`/`ObservationWriter` are actively emitting these (writer-side root cause unresolved); filter them from the JSON export before loading
- **`obs-api` or VKB still reflecting stale/collapsed graph after rehydration**: both services hold in-memory state; restart both after rehydration to pick up the recovered graph
- **`isProject` static ESM import failure in `mcp-servers:semantic-analysis` container**: open unresolved issue introduced during Phase 57; container fails on `isProject` import; no confirmed workaround

id: Mention-Candidate System — Filtering Semantics and Data Quality
topic: Mention-Candidate System — Filtering Semantics and Data Quality
summary:
## Purpose
Controls which graph entities are admitted into the mention-candidate pool used during wave-analysis mention-classification. Candidates are assembled by `loadMentionCandidates` (filtering on `ontologyClass`) and resolved through `findByOntologyClass`, which applies OR-gate semantics. A widespread `ontologyClass` tagging error contaminates the pool and produces false mention edges.

## Architecture
**Candidate pool assembly (`loadMentionCandidates`):**
- Selects entities using `ontologyClass` as the filter key — not `entityType`
- Current pool: 802 nodes; intended to contain L1/L2/L3 records only
- Actual contamination: 74 Insights tagged `ontologyClass='Detail'` (instead of `'Insight'`), plus 16 Processes, 15 Observations, and 11 Containers with incorrect class tags — all admitted alongside legitimate L1/L2/L3 entries

**`findByOntologyClass` OR-gate semantics:**
- Resolves via `entityType OR ontologyClass` match — not strict class-only filtering
- `isActive` filter: entities without a `validUntil` timestamp unconditionally pass; all 100 current Insight entities lack `validUntil`, so all are always reachable
- CR-01's claim that the bridge reached only "22 entities" was a misreading of this OR-gate; the bridge reaches all 100 Insights without scope correction

**False-edge impact from contamination:**
- Contaminated pool generates self-referential `Insight→Insight` mention edges and false `Insight→Process` edges
- Total false-edge noise: ~1.2% of all edges — considered acceptable; `ontologyClass` correction deprioritized
- When re-running coverage analysis, filter to L1/L2/L3 records only to exclude self-referential noise from metrics

**Coverage metric:**
- Tracks Insights with zero mention-candidate coverage ("uncovered Insights")
- Pre-bridge baseline: 37 uncovered Insights; post-bridge execution: 25 uncovered — improvement confirmed from live pipeline changes (bridge execution + writer-path emission activation)

## Key Files
- `~/Agentic/coding/src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js` — contains `loadMentionCandidates` (filters by `ontologyClass`); calls `findByOntologyClass`; Insight synthesis entry point
- `58-HUMAN-UAT.md` — SC#1 bridge scope verification and coverage improvement tracking

## Usage
The 802-node candidate pool is assembled at wave-analysis runtime. Coverage metrics (uncovered Insight count) verify that bridge execution and writer-path emission activation are taking effect. Filter coverage analysis to L1/L2/L3 records only to avoid inflating uncovered-Insight counts with self-referential noise. The `ontologyClass` data quality fix (retagging 74 Insights from `'Detail'` to `'Insight'`) is tracked but deprioritized.

## Troubleshooting
- **Insights appearing in their own candidate pool**: 74 Insights carry `ontologyClass='Detail'`; they enter the pool and match against themselves; mitigate immediately by filtering coverage analysis to L1/L2/L3 records; permanent fix requires retagging those 74 entities to `ontologyClass='Insight'`
- **False `Insight→Insight` or `Insight→Process` mention edges in graph**: caused by ontologyClass mistagging; current impact ~1.2% of total edges; deprioritized pending further real-world impact assessment
- **Bridge appears to reach fewer than 100 Insights**: verify OR-gate semantics in `findByOntologyClass` (`entityType OR ontologyClass`); confirm all Insights lack `validUntil` (so all pass `isActive`); all 100 Insights are reachable — any lower count is a filter or analysis error
- **Coverage metrics not improving after bridge execution**: confirm writer-path emission was activated before the bridge run; expected post-bridge baseline is ~25 uncovered (down from 37)
- **`loadMentionCandidates` admitting unexpected entity types**: the filter operates on `ontologyClass`, not `entityType`; entities with wrong `ontologyClass` values will be admitted regardless of their structural type

topic: Knowledge Graph Semantic Edges — Online Ingest Pipeline
summary:
## Purpose
Observation nodes produced by the online ingest pipeline are persisted with only a single outgoing relation (`capturedBy → LiveLoggingSystem`). Semantic-content edges (`discusses`, `mentions`, `references`) that should link each Observation to the specific Insights and Components it references are not written. This gap causes all graph traversal from Observation nodes to terminate at `LiveLoggingSystem`, preventing meaningful ancestry resolution and knowledge graph attribution.

## Architecture
- **Affected data scope**: all Observation nodes produced by Pipeline 1 (online ingest); each carries exactly one outgoing relation: `capturedBy → LiveLoggingSystem`; no semantic-content edges are present on any node
- **Missing edge types**: `discusses`, `mentions`, `references` — semantic-content relations that should link each Observation to relevant Insights and Components in the knowledge graph
- **Three implicated code locations**:
  - **`persistence-agent.ts`** (lines 928–944) — writes relations per Observation; currently writes only `capturedBy`; the primary target for adding semantic-content edge writes
  - **`coordinator.ts`** — suspected of not classifying Observations against the Insight/Component graph before or during persistence; classification is a prerequisite for resolving correct edge targets (without it, the persistence layer has no target IDs to write)
  - **`insight-generation-agent.ts`** — suspected of not creating back-edges from generated Insights to their source Observations; back-edges are required for bidirectional traversal
- **Downstream symptom**: BFS ancestor traversal in the viewer (and any other graph query) returns `LiveLoggingSystem` for every Observation because no alternate path exists in the stored data; the traversal logic is correct — the data is deficient
- **Ingest path context**: online ingest flows `ObservationConsolidator.js` → VKB HTTP PUT with `source: 'online'`; the gap is in what relations are written during this path, not in the transport mechanism itself

## Key Files
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/persistence-agent.ts` — lines 928–944; primary fix target; needs to write `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` in addition to `capturedBy`
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/coordinator.ts` — Observation classification logic; must classify Observations against the Insight/Component graph to produce valid edge targets before persistence
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/agents/insight-generation-agent.ts` — Insight generation; needs back-edge writes from each generated Insight to its source Observation(s)
- `~/Agentic/coding/src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js` — Pipeline 1 orchestrator; VKB HTTP PUT entry point
- `general.json` (e.g. `.data/exports/knowledge-export/general.json`) — raw graph export; use `jq` to confirm absence of `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` edges with Observation sources before and after fix

## Usage
This is an open defect in the online ingest pipeline. All downstream consumers — viewer graph traversal, VKB <USER_ID_REDACTED>, timeline-to-entity attribution — receive degraded results until semantic-content edges are added. The recommended fix sequence: (1) audit `coordinator.ts` to understand or implement Observation classification against the Insight/Component graph; (2) pass resolved target IDs into `persistence-agent.ts` for writing semantic-content edges; (3) add back-edge writes in `insight-generation-agent.ts`. Do not modify traversal logic in `ancestry.ts` or viewer components to work around this defect — the fix must be applied upstream.

## Troubleshooting
- **Confirming edge absence**: query `general.json` with `jq` — e.g. `jq '[.edges[] | select(.relation == "discusses" or .relation == "mentions" or .relation == "references")] | length'` (adapt field names to actual export schema); expected result is `0` until the fix is applied
- **Graph traversal always returns `LiveLoggingSystem` for Observation nodes**: confirmed data defect; do not attempt to fix by adjusting BFS direction or traversal logic — add the missing edges upstream
- **Identifying correct edge targets**: `coordinator.ts` classification determines which Insight/Component IDs are semantically linked to a given Observation; audit this logic first to ensure targets are well-defined before writing edges in `persistence-agent.ts`
- **Back-edge direction for `insight-generation-agent.ts`**: edges should point Insight → source Observation(s); verify directionality before adding to avoid inverting the semantic relationship
- **Fix interacts with VKB viewer rendering**: after adding semantic-content edges, verify that the VKB graph viewer's filter and legend logic handles the new edge types correctly; `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` are not currently in the viewer's declared legend

metadata.scope: Missing `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` edges from Observation nodes in the semantic-analysis ingest pipeline
metadata.topic: Knowledge Graph Semantic Edges — Online Ingest Pipeline
metadata.summary:
## Purpose
Observation nodes produced by the online ingest pipeline are persisted with only a single outgoing relation (`capturedBy → LiveLoggingSystem`). Semantic-content edges (`discusses`, `mentions`, `references`) that should link each Observation to the specific Insights and Components it references are not written. This gap causes all graph traversal from Observation nodes to terminate at `LiveLoggingSystem`, preventing meaningful ancestry resolution and knowledge graph attribution.

## Architecture
- **Affected data scope**: all Observation nodes produced by Pipeline 1 (online ingest); each carries exactly one outgoing relation: `capturedBy → LiveLoggingSystem`; no semantic-content edges are present on any node
- **Missing edge types**: `discusses`, `mentions`, `references` — semantic-content relations that should link each Observation to relevant Insights and Components in the knowledge graph
- **Three implicated code locations**:
  - **`persistence-agent.ts`** (lines 928–944) — writes relations per Observation; currently writes only `capturedBy`; the primary target for adding semantic-content edge writes
  - **`coordinator.ts`** — suspected of not classifying Observations against the Insight/Component graph before or during persistence; classification is a prerequisite for resolving correct edge targets (without it, the persistence layer has no target IDs to write)
  - **`insight-generation-agent.ts`** — suspected of not creating back-edges from generated Insights to their source Observations; back-edges are required for bidirectional traversal
- **Downstream symptom**: BFS ancestor traversal in the viewer (and any other graph query) returns `LiveLoggingSystem` for every Observation because no alternate path exists in the stored data; the traversal logic is correct — the data is deficient
- **Ingest path context**: online ingest flows `ObservationConsolidator.js` → VKB HTTP PUT with `source: 'online'`; the gap is in what relations are written during this path, not in the transport mechanism itself

## Key Files
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/persistence-agent.ts` — lines 928–944; primary fix target; needs to write `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` in addition to `capturedBy`

- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/agents/insight-generation-agent.ts` — Insight generation; needs back-edge writes from each generated Insight to its source Observation(s)

- **Back-edge direction for `insight-generation-agent.ts`**: edges should point Insight → source Observation(s); verify directionality before adding to avoid inverting the semantic relationship

topic: Git Security — OAuth Token Scrubbing and Redaction Patterns
summary:
## Purpose
Prevents OAuth tokens and personal identifiers from persisting in git history or the observations database, and configures ongoing redaction for future writes.

## Architecture
Two distinct layers:
1. **Git history scrubbing**: Uses `git-filter-repo` with a replacements file to rewrite all commits and remove token strings matching `gho_`, `ghp_`, `github_pat_` prefixes. After rewriting, force-push to remote.
2. **Runtime redaction**: `ConfigurableRedactor` applies patterns from `redaction-patterns.json` to observation content before DB write. Patterns cover user IDs, email addresses, file paths, and OAuth token prefixes.

Retroactive DB cleanup (for data written before the redactor was active) requires a direct SQL update against the `messages` column.

## Key Files
- `redaction-patterns.json` — pattern list for `ConfigurableRedactor`; must include `gho_`, `ghp_`, `github_pat_` for OAuth tokens
- `~/Agentic/coding/.specstory/config/redaction-patterns.json` — specstory-specific redaction config
- `ObservationWriter.js` — applies `ConfigurableRedactor` on new observation writes
- `.env` — may contain redaction-related environment variables

## Usage
`ConfigurableRedactor` runs automatically on every new observation write. For historical cleanup, run targeted SQL updates or a script against the observations SQLite DB. For git history cleanup, run `git-filter-repo --replace-text <replacements-file>` then force-push.

## Troubleshooting
- **Raw user IDs or emails appear in stored observations**: Data was written before the redactor was configured; run retroactive SQL cleanup on the `messages` column
- **OAuth token still found in git history after filter-repo**: Verify the replacements file covers the exact token string; re-run `git log -S <token>` to confirm removal before force-pushing
- **Second force-push appears to fail after filter-repo**: The first push likely succeeded; verify remote state with `git fetch` before retrying

topic: Pre-Commit Hook — KB File Handling
summary:
## Purpose
Validates commits before they are recorded. The "OKB BASELINE GUARD" hook enforces that KB export files (`.data/exports/knowledge-export/` or `.data/knowledge-export/`) are not staged alongside non-KB changes, preventing accidental mixed commits from polluting KB history.

## Architecture
- Hook lives inside the `operational-knowledge-management` submodule (referenced from the parent repo as a submodule pointer)
- **Mixed commits** (KB files + other artifacts) trigger the hook's warning/block path
- **KB-only commits** bypass the hook entirely — this is the intended design
- The parent repository tracks the submodule pointer; divergence between local and remote submodule commits requires `git pull --rebase origin main` to re-align cleanly

## Key Files
- Pre-commit hook script inside `operational-knowledge-management` submodule — contains the KB-file bypass conditional
- Parent repo submodule pointer — updated when submodule commits are made

## Usage
Runs automatically on every `git commit` in the submodule or parent repo context. When blocked, the fix is to unstage KB files and commit them independently (KB-only commit), then commit remaining changes separately.

## Troubleshooting
- **Commit blocked by "OKB BASELINE GUARD"**: KB files are staged alongside non-KB changes — unstage KB files (`git restore --staged <kb-files>`), commit the non-KB changes first, then commit the KB files alone
- **KB-only commit still blocked**: Verify the bypass conditional is present in the hook; if absent, the fix was not applied to the submodule's hook file
- **Submodule diverged from remote**: Run `git pull --rebase origin main` inside the submodule directory to replay local commits on top of remote history without creating a merge commit; then update the parent repo's submodule pointer

topic: Unified Viewer Graph Layout
summary:
## Purpose
`CollectiveKnowledge` is the designated system root node in the knowledge graph, providing the single unified entry point for root-to-leaf traversal across all project sub-graphs. The complete traversal chain (`CollectiveKnowledge → Project → Component → SubComponent → Detail`) depends on directed outgoing edges from this node to each of the four Project nodes being present.

## Architecture
- **Root position**: `CollectiveKnowledge` sits at ring `r=0` (the Project ring) in the viewer's radial layout
- **Traversal dependency**: graph traversal from root through sub-graphs requires all four outgoing edges to Project nodes to exist; without them, the graph has no unified root and traversal is broken
- **Orphan risk**: if `CollectiveKnowledge`'s outgoing edges are absent at render time (e.g., after a migration, bulk-delete, or orphan-purge pass), `graph-builder.ts` zero-edge detection misidentifies it as an orphan and seeds it at `r=60` instead of `r=0`, displacing it from the graph center and severing the traversal chain
- **`scripts/link-collective-to-projects.mjs`**: idempotent repair script that programmatically establishes directed edges from `CollectiveKnowledge` to each Project node; safe to re-run — will not duplicate existing edges

## Key Files
- `scripts/link-collective-to-projects.mjs` — restores directed edges from `CollectiveKnowledge` to each of the four Project nodes; idempotent
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/graph/graph-builder.ts` — orphan detection seeds zero-edge nodes at `r=60`; `CollectiveKnowledge` must have outgoing edges before this runs to seat correctly at `r=0`

## Usage
After any graph migration, bulk-delete, or orphan-cleanup operation, verify that `CollectiveKnowledge` still holds outgoing edges to all four Project nodes. Under normal operation no ongoing maintenance is required. If traversal from root appears broken, run `link-collective-to-projects.mjs` and then hard-reload the viewer.

## Troubleshooting
- **Graph traversal broken from root (no path to any Project node)**: Run `link-collective-to-projects.mjs` to restore all four directed edges; hard-reload the viewer afterward
- **`CollectiveKnowledge` renders at orphan position (`r=60`) rather than graph center (`r=0`)**: Zero outgoing edges were detected at render time — run `link-collective-to-projects.mjs` first, then reload; the graph-builder will then seat it correctly at `r=0`
- **Some Project nodes unreachable from root while others are not**: Partial edge loss — `link-collective-to-projects.mjs` is idempotent; re-run it to restore all edges without affecting existing ones

topic: Unified Viewer — Filter Infrastructure
summary:
## Purpose
Manages user-controlled filter state for the unified viewer, enabling graph entities and the footer node count to be narrowed by multiple dimensions: Learning Source, Layer, Teams, and HideDocs. Provides a central store (`viewer-store.ts`), individual React filter components, and a `visibleCount` computation in the main route component that enforces all active dimensions simultaneously.

## Architecture
- **`viewer-store.ts`**: central state store for all viewer filter state; contains filter state fields and mutation functions per dimension; Learning Source (Online/Batch/Combined) and Teams filters are distinct state slices alongside Layer and HideDocs
- **Filter components**: each dimension has a dedicated React component reading from and writing to the store
  - `LearningSourceFilter.tsx`: Learning Source filter UI (Online/Batch/Combined)
  - `TeamsFilter.tsx`: Teams filter UI; **known bug** — toggling produces incorrect filter state; targeted fix is queued but not yet applied
- **`UnifiedViewer.tsx` — visibleCount**: the main route component contains the `visibleCount` computation that gates the footer node count and D3 graph visibility; it must respect **all** active filter dimensions — LearningSource, Layer, Teams, and HideDocs; omitting any dimension causes the footer and graph to appear unresponsive to that control
- **Open issue — checkbox visual artifact**: Radix `CheckIcon` with class `size-3.5` produces a crossed/struck-through visual on checkboxes; root cause is ambiguous between a CSS sizing problem and upstream Layer filter logic breakage causing corrupt render state
- **Open issue — Layer filter logic**: a logic breakage in the Layer filter was flagged during the checkbox investigation; not yet resolved

## Key Files
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/store/viewer-store.ts` (approx.) — all filter state fields and mutations; Learning Source and Teams state slices
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/routes/UnifiedViewer.tsx` — main route component; `visibleCount` must gate on LearningSource, Layer, Teams, and HideDocs
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/components/LearningSourceFilter.tsx` (approx.) — Learning Source filter UI
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/components/TeamsFilter.tsx` (approx.) — Teams filter UI; **known bug**: toggle produces incorrect state; fix queued

## Usage
Filter components are rendered in the unified viewer's filter panel. User interactions write to `viewer-store.ts`. The `visibleCount` in `UnifiedViewer.tsx` reads all filter dimensions and drives the footer count and D3 graph visibility. Adding a new filter requires: (1) a new state field + mutation in `viewer-store.ts`, (2) a corresponding filter component, and (3) adding the new dimension to `visibleCount` in `UnifiedViewer.tsx`.

## Troubleshooting
- **Footer count or D3 graph doesn't respond to LearningSource / Teams / HideDocs filter**: `visibleCount` in `UnifiedViewer.tsx` is not gating on that dimension — verify all four (LearningSource, Layer, Teams, HideDocs) are included in the count logic
- **Teams filter toggle produces incorrect selection state**: confirmed bug in `TeamsFilter.tsx`; fix queued but not applied — results will not accurately reflect toggle until resolved
- **Checkbox renders with crossed or struck-through appearance**: test with an alternate size class for Radix `CheckIcon`; also verify whether the Layer filter logic breakage causes compound corrupt render state
- **Layer filter not applying to graph**: check the Layer state field/mutation in `viewer-store.ts` and confirm it gates entity inclusion downstream; diagnose independently from the visual artifact
- **New filter selection not reflected in graph or footer**: verify the filter mutation output reaches both the graph data pipeline and the `visibleCount` gate in `UnifiedViewer.tsx`

topic: Knowledge Graph API and Working Memory Service
summary:
## Purpose
Exposes the project's knowledge graph (Memgraph) entities via a REST API so that the working memory module and other consumers can retrieve structured project context (Projects, Components, SubComponents, etc.) for injection into agent context windows.

## Architecture
- **Entity API**: `/api/entities` route in `api-routes.js`; accepts `types` (plural, CSV) parameter — e.g., `?types=Component,SubComponent`; also accepts legacy `type` (singular) for backward compatibility
- **KG contents**: 673 correctly-typed entities — 1 Project (Coding), 8 Component, 353 SubComponent, 310 Detail, 1 System
- **GraphDatabaseService deduplication**: Project nodes were previously omitted from `?types=Project` results due to second-pass deduplication logic in `GraphDatabaseService.js`; fix requires Docker rebuild to deploy
- **Client-side leak prevention**: `working-memory.js` filters out Project/System nodes that leak into component-type result lists
- **Docker deployment**: `coding-services` container is NOT bind-mounted; changes to `GraphDatabaseService.js` require a full Docker rebuild and restart

## Key Files
- `api-routes.js` — `/api/entities` route; accepts both `type` and `types` parameters
- `GraphDatabaseService.js` — executes Cypher <USER_ID_REDACTED>; deduplication logic must not drop Project nodes
- `src/retrieval/working-memory.js` — calls `/api/entities?types=Component` and filters results client-side to exclude Project and System nodes

## Usage
Called by `working-memory.js` at injection time to build the 300-token KG prefix. Also available for direct querying during debugging or cleanup scripts. The `types=` (plural) parameter is the canonical form; `type=` (singular) is supported for backward compatibility only.

## Troubleshooting
- **`working-memory.js` returns 0 components**: Verify the API call uses `types=` (plural); `type=` singular was the original bug
- **Project entity missing from `?types=Project` results**: Second-pass dedup in `GraphDatabaseService.js` was stripping Project nodes — verify the fix is deployed (requires Docker rebuild)
- **System entity appearing in component lists**: Client-side filter in `working-memory.js` must explicitly exclude `Project` and `System` node types
- **Changes to `GraphDatabaseService.js` not reflected**: Container is not bind-mounted; run `docker-compose build && docker-compose up -d` to redeploy

metadata.topic: Knowledge Graph API and Working Memory Service
metadata.summary:
## Purpose
Exposes the project's knowledge graph (Memgraph) entities via a REST API so that the working memory module and other consumers can retrieve structured project context (Projects, Components, SubComponents, etc.) for injection into agent context windows.

## Architecture
- **Entity API**: `/api/entities` route in `api-routes.js`; accepts `types` (plural, CSV) parameter — e.g., `?types=Component,SubComponent`; also accepts legacy `type` (singular) for backward compatibility
- **KG contents**: 673 correctly-typed entities — 1 Project (Coding), 8 Component, 353 SubComponent, 310 Detail, 1 System
- **GraphDatabaseService deduplication**: Project nodes were previously omitted from `?types=Project` results due to second-pass deduplication logic in `GraphDatabaseService.js`; fix requires Docker rebuild to deploy
- **Client-side leak prevention**: `working-memory.js` filters out Project/System nodes that leak into component-type result lists
- **Docker deployment**: `coding-services` container is NOT bind-mounted; changes to `GraphDatabaseService.js` require a full Docker rebuild and restart

## Key Files
- `api-routes.js` — `/api/entities` route; accepts both `type` and `types` parameters
- `GraphDatabaseService.js` — executes Cypher <USER_ID_REDACTED>; deduplication logic must not drop Project nodes
- `src/retrieval/working-memory.js` — calls `/api/entities?types=Component` and filters results client-side to exclude Project and System nodes

## Usage
Called by `working-memory.js` at injection time to build the 300-token KG prefix. Also available for direct querying during debugging or cleanup scripts. The `types=` (plural) parameter is the canonical form; `type=` (singular) is supported for backward compatibility only.

## Troubleshooting
- **`working-memory.js` returns 0 components**: Verify the API call uses `types=` (plural); `type=` singular was the original bug
- **Project entity missing from `?types=Project` results**: Second-pass dedup in `GraphDatabaseService.js` was stripping Project nodes — verify the fix is deployed (requires Docker rebuild)
- **System entity appearing in component lists**: Client-side filter in `working-memory.js` must explicitly exclude `Project` and `System` node types
- **Changes to `GraphDatabaseService.js` not reflected**: Container is not bind-mounted; run `docker-compose build && docker-compose up -d` to redeploy
metadata.scope: `api-routes.js` `/api/entities` endpoint and `working-memory.js` — Memgraph entity retrieval for the 300-token KG context prefix

topic: LSL Log Path Layout — Migration to .specstory/history/logs/
summary:
## Purpose
Documents the git workflow hazards that arise when background monitoring processes (ETM, km-core, live exporters) are active alongside git operations. Covers both the background-process re-staging race condition and the commit hygiene rules for live-updated data files.

## Architecture
**Race condition mechanism:**
- ETM and similar background agents monitor transcript and source files; when a file write occurs between `git restore --staged <file>` and the subsequent `git commit`, the background process silently re-stages the file
- Re-staging is invisible — pre-commit hooks are not involved; `git reflog` shows the commit as having included the file normally
- Affected files observed: `src/live-logging/ObservationWriter.js`, `scripts/configure-wave-analysis-routing.sh`, `scripts/enhanced-transcript-monitor.js`, `ObservationWriter.pre-llm-dedup.test.js`
- Leading indicator: explicitly unstaged files reappear in `git diff --staged` before the commit is made

**Recovery and prevention:**
- **Recovery**: `git reset --soft HEAD~1` — non-destructive; preserves the index and working tree
- **Definitive fix**: `git commit --only <file1> <file2> ...` (`-o`) — bypasses the staging index entirely; immune to re-staging that occurs during the unstage→commit window

**Live data file commit rules:**
- `.data/observation-export/` JSONs (`insights.json`, `metadata.json`, `observations.json`) — continuously rewritten by the live exporter; any commit is a point-in-time snapshot; safe to commit deliberately when a snapshot is desired
- `.data/knowledge-graph/exports/general.json` — permanently churning due to the running `km-core` process; must be left uncommitted; add to `.gitignore` or habitually skip during staging
- `.claude/settings.local.json` — may accumulate SSH probe permissions and diagnostic entries during session work; commit only when changes are intentional

## Key Files
- `.data/observation-export/insights.json`, `.data/observation-export/metadata.json`, `.data/observation-export/observations.json` — live-rewritten observation exports; point-in-time snapshots only
- `.data/knowledge-graph/exports/general.json` — km-core live export; do not commit
- `.claude/settings.local.json` — local Claude settings; may contain SSH probe permissions added during diagnostic sessions
- `.git/hooks/` — inspect here to rule out pre-commit hook involvement in unexpected staging behavior

## Usage
Use `git commit --only <files>` (`-o`) as the standard commit method whenever ETM or other background monitoring processes are active — which is effectively always during normal development. For data file commits: explicitly decide at commit time whether an observation-export snapshot is desired; never stage `general.json`. Use `git reset --soft HEAD~1` to safely undo a mixed commit without losing staged changes. Reserve the `git add` + `git commit` (index-based) workflow only for sessions where background processes are confirmed stopped.

## Troubleshooting
- **Files reappear in commit after explicit `git restore --staged`**: Background ETM process re-staged them during the unstage→commit window — switch to `git commit --only <specific-files>` to bypass the index
- **Pre-commit hook suspected**: Inspect `.git/hooks/` for executable scripts; an empty or non-executable hooks directory rules out hook involvement and points to the re-staging race
- **`git reset --soft HEAD~1` appears to have lost changes**: `--soft` preserves the index and working tree; changes are still staged — verify with `git status`; `--hard` would have discarded changes
- **Determining which files belong in the commit after a mixed commit**: Run `git reset --soft HEAD~1`, then `git status`; selectively unstage unintended files with `git restore --staged <file>` before committing with `git commit --only`
- **`general.json` perpetually appearing in `git status`**: Expected behavior — km-core rewrites this file continuously; do not commit it; consider adding it to `.gitignore`
- **Working tree never fully clean due to observation export churn**: Normal when the live exporter is running; accept the permanent dirty state and use explicit snapshots only when a point-in-time record is needed

topic: Teams Channel Message Extraction via Graph API
summary:
## Purpose
Enables full retrieval of Teams channel message history for reporting, analysis, or archival via the <COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED> Graph API.

## Architecture
- **Auth:** Device-code OAuth flow — requires browser-based sign-in; tokens expire and must be refreshed per session
- **API:** <COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED> Graph API `ListChannelMessages` endpoint, accessed via MCP proxy or CLI wrapper (`cmd_channel_messages`)
- **Pagination:** Graph API hard-caps at 50 messages per request; `cmd_channel_messages` follows `nextLink`/`skipToken` tokens in a loop until the full history is exhausted

## Key Files
- `cmd_channel_messages` — CLI wrapper handling pagination and authentication; patched to follow `nextLink`/`skipToken` automatically

## Usage
Invoked during channel reporting cycles to pull the full message corpus. Output feeds the link resolution and report generation pipeline. All Teams access must go through the Graph API — no direct Teams client access.

## Troubleshooting
- **Only 20 messages returned:** Default `--limit` is 20; raise to 50 (API maximum per single request)
- **History truncates at a past date:** Pagination loop not active — verify `nextLink`/`skipToken` following is enabled in `cmd_channel_messages`
- **Auth failures:** Re-run the device-code OAuth flow to obtain a fresh token

metadata.topic: Teams Channel Message Extraction via Graph API

topic: Rapid-Toolkit Repository
summary:
## Purpose
A catalog of 142+ reusable automation components for enterprise workflows, covering Jira, Confluence, ServiceNow, CodeBeamer, RCA diagnostics, and <COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED> 365/Teams integration.

## Architecture
- **33 tools:** Discrete executable automations
- **15 plugins:** Composable integrations (e.g., `m365cli`, webhook Adaptive Cards)
- **94+ skills:** AI agent-callable skill definitions
- Teams/M365 integration handled via `m365cli` bindings and webhook-based Adaptive Card posting

## Key Files
- `~/Agentic/_work/rapid-automations/integrations/rapid-toolkit/` — repository root
- Plugin manifests — describe available plugins and entry points
- `README` — top-level inventory and usage overview

## Usage
Used by agents and automation scripts to compose enterprise workflows. Skills are callable by AI agents; tools and plugins can be invoked via CLI or scripted pipelines. Covers platform APIs, product owner workflows, PowerPoint generation, and traceability via CodeBeamer.

## Troubleshooting
- Inventory via directory traversal + README + plugin manifest scanning
- No modification should occur during discovery/read-only phases
- For Teams integration, verify `m365cli` authentication state before invoking M365-dependent tools
